mappings.feature: Patient billing / invoicing (charge lines, invoice lifecycle, balance)
mappings.license_status: gpl (OpenHospital) / agpl (OpenMRS) - reference-only; do not copy. Local already first-party.
mappings.reason: Local invoice/ChargeLine/InvoiceStatus(draft/issued/partiallyPaid/paid/voided)/balanceMinor model in healthstack-billing.tsp already matches or exceeds OpenHospital's Bill/BillItems shape and persists real rows (billing_invoice table). Money is integer-minor with fail-closed Zod (SafeMinorNonNeg, #369) - stronger than OpenHospital's float prices. Source value is the price-list/charge-master concept (see gap row), not the invoice CRUD itself.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: OpenHospital's PatientNewBill is a cashier/admin desk form (org desk first). Local already re-centers: patient-app billing.tsx shows the person their own invoices + outstanding balance as a journey card; front-office is the staff mirror over the same contract.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app/app/(tabs)/billing.tsx + app/healthstack-billing/[id] detail: my bills, what I owe, what insurance covered.
mappings.management_surface: front-office billing screen: full invoice list/detail, void, balance, per-encounter charge lines (PatientBillingSummary, listInvoices, GET /patients/{id}/billing).
mappings.simplification_note: One invoice contract + two re-centered surfaces vs OpenHospital's three separate Java/TS browsers (BillBrowser, PatientBillEdit, billingActivity).
mappings.no_loss_check: Invoice lifecycle, charge lines, partial-pay/balance, void, per-patient summary all present locally; nothing from OpenHospital Bill/BillItems is lost.
mappings.risk: low

mappings.feature: Charge master / fee schedule (price lists, service pricing, contract rates)
mappings.license_status: gpl - port-adapt the data model + rules, do not copy Java verbatim.
mappings.reason: Local has NO price list / fee schedule: estimates in healthstack-billing.tsp take plannedCodes[] but there is no priced charge master to compute estimatedTotalMinor from real rates, and invoice ChargeLine.unitAmountMinor has no authoritative source. OpenHospital PricesList/PricesOthers + multi-price-list-per-payer is the canonical model. Needed by both estimate (copay) and claim charge accuracy.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source price lists are an admin maintenance grid. Re-center as the engine behind the patient's up-front cost estimate ('what will this visit cost me') - the person never sees the grid, only the resolved price.
mappings.person_surface: Feeds patient-app estimate ('your estimated copay') - person sees the resolved number, not the schedule.
mappings.management_surface: Billing-admin fee-schedule maintenance (per-payer contracted rates, effective dates) - full management capability preserved.
mappings.simplification_note: Single fee-schedule entity keyed (tenant, payerPlanId, code, effectiveDate) replaces OpenHospital's split PricesList + PricesOthers + per-list item tables.
mappings.no_loss_check: Multiple price lists, per-item override, 'other' charges, effective dating all captured in the new entity; nothing dropped.
mappings.risk: low

mappings.feature: Insurance claim generation - X12 5010 837P / 837I (professional + institutional EDI)
mappings.license_status: gpl - the X12 5010 segment layout is an ANSI standard (not copyrightable); port-adapt the LOGIC, write fresh TS. Do NOT copy OpenEMR PHP verbatim.
mappings.reason: This is the single highest-value gap. Local 'submit claim' (POST /claims/:id/submit) only sets status='submitted' - NO actual X12 file is produced. OpenEMR Claim.php (2287 LoC) + X125010837P/I (2865 LoC) is a complete, production 837 professional + institutional generator with ISA/GS/ST envelopes, batch control numbers, loops 2000/2300/2400, subscriber/payer/provider segments. Re-implement as a TS X12 837 emitter; the standard segments make it portable. healthstack-claims-service (currently chart-only scaffold) is the owner.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: EDI is pure back-office (it never had a person surface). Person-centric reshape = the patient sees claim STATUS ('submitted to BlueCross', 'in adjudication', 'paid') as a journey timeline, never the X12. The org's EDI process is satisfied as a consequence.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app claim-status timeline card: 'your claim was sent to <payer>, expected response <date>'.
mappings.management_surface: Billing-admin claim batch console: generate 837P/I, view raw X12, batch control numbers, 997/999 acknowledgments, resubmit - full RCM management capability.
mappings.simplification_note: Generator-first: a single @curaos/x12 SDK package emits 837P + 837I from the canonical Claim contract, so the service mold never hand-writes EDI; future payers/forms extend the SDK, not the service.
mappings.no_loss_check: 837P, 837I, ISA/GS envelopes, batch control numbers, subscriber/payer/provider/service loops, NDC/modifiers all preserved (filed as backlog if any segment slips a milestone). Nothing from OpenEMR's claim generator is cut.
mappings.risk: high - X12 correctness is payer-tested; needs clearinghouse conformance test fixtures.

mappings.feature: Paper claim forms - HCFA/CMS-1500 + UB-04 (PDF generation)
mappings.license_status: gpl - CMS-1500/UB-04 are government forms (field layout is public); port-adapt field-mapping logic, fresh TS PDF render.
mappings.reason: Many small/secondary payers still require paper CMS-1500 / UB-04. Local has zero paper output. OpenEMR Hcfa1500.php (762 LoC) + UB04 generators map claim fields to box coordinates. Same canonical Claim contract that feeds X12 feeds the PDF, so it belongs in the @curaos/x12 (rename: @curaos/claim-forms) SDK as a sibling emitter.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: No person surface (paper is for the payer). Patient-centric angle: the person can download a copy of their own submitted CMS-1500 as part of their record.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app: download my claim form (transparency over my own billing).
mappings.management_surface: Billing-admin: print/queue CMS-1500 + UB-04 for paper payers; same claim object as EDI.
mappings.simplification_note: One Claim contract, two emitters (X12 + PDF) in one SDK; no duplicate field maps.
mappings.no_loss_check: CMS-1500 + UB-04 both covered; no paper-form capability lost vs OpenEMR.
mappings.risk: medium - box-coordinate fidelity must match payer OCR.

mappings.feature: Real-time insurance eligibility / benefits - X12 270/271
mappings.license_status: gpl - 270/271 is ANSI X12 standard; port-adapt logic, fresh TS.
mappings.reason: Local POST /claims/:id/eligibility only flips a stored eligibility status string (Zod-validates an empty body, mutates the claim) - it does NOT call a payer. billing_claim.eligibility is a manual field. OpenEMR EDI270.php (1162 LoC) builds the real 270 inquiry (ISA/GS/subscriber loops) and parses the 271 response (active coverage, copay/coinsurance/deductible). This turns the local stub into a real benefits check that also feeds accurate copay estimates.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: OpenEMR's eligibility is a staff 'check coverage' button. Re-center: the person's check-in journey auto-runs eligibility and shows THEM 'your insurance is active, your copay is $X' before the visit - the staff verification step happens silently behind that.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app check-in: 'coverage active, estimated copay $X today'.
mappings.management_surface: Front-office staff eligibility panel: 271 detail (plan, deductible met, coverage dates), manual re-check, payer connection config.
mappings.simplification_note: Eligibility result drives the Estimate contract directly (estimatedCoverageMinor/estimatedCopayMinor already exist in healthstack-billing.tsp), so one round-trip serves both the person estimate and the management panel.
mappings.no_loss_check: 270 inquiry, 271 parse (active coverage, copay, coinsurance, deductible, coverage dates) all preserved; manual override retained for offline payers.
mappings.risk: medium - needs payer/clearinghouse connectivity config + secrets.

mappings.feature: ERA / 835 remittance auto-posting (payment + adjustment reconciliation)
mappings.license_status: gpl - 835 is ANSI X12; port-adapt the CLP/CAS/SVC loop logic, fresh TS.
mappings.reason: Local payment capture (capturePayment) only records a patient-side cash/card payment against an invoice. There is NO payer-side remittance ingest: no 835 parse, no auto-post of payer payments, no CARC/RARC adjustment posting, no payment-balance reconciliation. OpenEMR ParseERA.php (561 LoC) parses 835 CLP (claim payment) + CAS (claim adjustment) + SVC (service-line) loops and posts to ar_activity. SLEOB.php handles EOB + automatic secondary billing. This closes the revenue cycle (claim -> payer payment -> adjustment -> secondary).
mappings.person_centric_reshape: 835 posting is invisible org accounting. Person-centric outcome: the patient sees their balance UPDATE automatically when insurance pays ('insurance paid $X, your remaining responsibility is $Y') and a plain-language denial reason if denied - they never see CARC codes.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app: real-time 'insurance paid / you owe' after adjudication, plain-language adjustment reasons.
mappings.management_surface: Billing-admin ERA inbox: upload/auto-fetch 835, auto-post payments, review CAS adjustments, trigger secondary claim (SLEOB), denial workqueue.
mappings.simplification_note: ERA posting writes through to the SAME billing_invoice balance the patient surface reads, and posts the neutral ledger entry via accounting-core-service - one write-through path, no parallel reconciliation system.
mappings.no_loss_check: CLP claim payment, CAS adjustments (CARC/RARC), SVC service-line detail, payment imbalance correction, EOB, auto-secondary billing all preserved.
mappings.risk: high - financial correctness; needs balance-reconciliation tests against fixture 835 files.

mappings.feature: Claim denial management / revenue-cycle worklist (status, denial reason, AR aging, patient responsibility)
mappings.license_status: gpl - reference the worklist + aging + day-sheet concepts; logic is generic.
mappings.reason: Local billing_claim already carries status, eligibility, denial_reason, age_days, patient_responsibility, payer, cpt_codes (a real worklist projection - stronger UX than OpenHospital's BillBrowser). What's weak: denial_reason is a free-text field with no CARC/RARC code mapping, no resubmit/appeal workflow, and age_days is static (not a live AR-aging report). OpenEMR BillingReport + DaySheet + sl_eob_search provide the aging buckets + day-sheet reconciliation + denial follow-up patterns.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: OpenEMR billing_report is an A/R manager screen. Re-center: the person sees a single 'where is my claim' status; the denial/appeal workflow is the staff spine attached behind that status.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app claim-status timeline (submitted/adjudicating/paid/denied + plain-language reason).
mappings.management_surface: Front-office RCM worklist: AR aging buckets (0-30/31-60/61-90/90+), denial reason (CARC/RARC), resubmit/appeal action, day-sheet totals - full management capability.
mappings.simplification_note: Worklist is a read-model projection fed by the claim-lifecycle events (submitted/accepted/paid/denied) the X12 + 835 lanes already emit; no separate worklist write path.
mappings.no_loss_check: AR aging, denial reason, day-sheet reconciliation, resubmit/appeal all preserved; local worklist fields kept and enriched with CARC/RARC + live aging.
mappings.risk: low

mappings.feature: Insurance / coverage management (policies, payers, coverage order, FHIR Coverage)
mappings.license_status: gpl - FHIR Coverage is a published HL7 resource; port-adapt + emit FHIR-shaped contract.
mappings.reason: Local references payer (billing_claim.payer) + payerPlanId (in EstimateRequest) but has NO patient-coverage entity: no insurance policy on file, no primary/secondary/tertiary coverage order, no subscriber/member-id, no FHIR Coverage. OpenEMR InsuranceService + FhirCoverageService model patient insurance with coverage type ordering. This is the data eligibility (270/271), claims (837), and ERA (secondary billing) all depend on.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: OpenEMR insurance tab is an admin data-entry form. Re-center: the person manages THEIR OWN coverage in patient-app ('my insurance cards') - photo upload, primary/secondary - and staff verify it.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app: my insurance cards, add/update coverage, set primary.
mappings.management_surface: Front-office coverage verification + coverage-order management; FHIR Coverage export for interop.
mappings.simplification_note: Coverage modeled as FHIR Coverage from day one (generator-first contract) so eligibility/claims/ERA all consume one canonical shape; PHI stays in the healthstack overlay schema per the PHI-boundary rule.
mappings.no_loss_check: Policy, payer, subscriber/member-id, coverage order (primary/secondary/tertiary), coverage type, FHIR Coverage all preserved.
mappings.risk: medium - PHI boundary: coverage subject refs in overlay schema, neutral cores reference-only.

gaps.feature: X12 5010 837P/837I claim EDI generation engine
gaps.local_evidence: POST /claims/:id/submit in healthstack-billings.controller.ts only sets status='submitted'; no X12 produced. healthstack-claims-service is chart-only scaffold (README + Helm chart, no src/). billing_claim schema has cpt_codes/payer/charge but no EDI output, no batch control numbers.
gaps.external_evidence: OpenEMR Claim.php (2287 LoC) + X125010837P.php (1640) + X125010837I.php (1225) + GeneratorX12.php + BillingClaimBatchControlNumber.php = complete production 837 professional + institutional generator with ISA/GS/ST envelopes and 2000/2300/2400 loops.
gaps.best_source: openemr (GPL; X12 segments are ANSI standard, logic is portable)
gaps.why: Single highest-value RCM gap. Without real 837, claims cannot be sent to payers/clearinghouses - the revenue cycle is non-functional past 'submitted' status.
steps: 1. Author canonical Claim TypeSpec contract (claim, subscriber, payer, provider, service lines, NDC, modifiers) in healthstack-claims-service/specs/.
2. Create @curaos/x12 SDK package with an 837 emitter (port-adapt OpenEMR loop logic to TS); add ISA/GS envelope + batch control-number module.
3. Add claim-fixture conformance snapshot tests (sample 837P/837I vs known-good output) in the SDK.
4. Promote healthstack-claims-service from scaffold: wire submitClaim to call the SDK, persist generated X12 + batch, emit claim.submitted event.
5. Fold the emitter into the service generator mold so every future claim variant extends the SDK, not the service.
gaps.no_loss_check: 837P + 837I, envelopes, batch control numbers, subscriber/payer/provider/service loops, NDC/modifiers preserved; any segment that slips the milestone is filed forward as a critical follow-up, never dropped.

gaps.feature: ERA / 835 remittance auto-posting + secondary billing (SLEOB)
gaps.local_evidence: capturePayment only records patient-side payments; schema.ts has no remittance/adjustment table; no payer-payment posting path; denial_reason is free text with no CARC/RARC.
gaps.external_evidence: OpenEMR ParseERA.php (561 LoC) parses 835 CLP/CAS/SVC loops with payment-imbalance correction; SLEOB.php (304 LoC) does EOB + automatic secondary claim generation.
gaps.why: Closes the revenue cycle: without 835 posting, payer payments and adjustments are not reconciled and patient balances stay wrong. Drives the person-facing 'insurance paid / you owe' update.
steps: 1. Add RemittanceAdvice/Adjustment (CARC/RARC) models to the billing contract.
2. Add 835 parser to @curaos/x12 SDK (CLP/CAS/SVC loops, imbalance correction); snapshot-test against fixture 835.
3. Add remittance + adjustment migrations; write-through to billing_invoice balance and post neutral ledger entry via accounting-core-service.
4. Port SLEOB: on primary EOB, auto-generate secondary 837 via the claims service.
5. Surface plain-language adjustment reasons on patient-app; CARC/RARC detail on front-office.
gaps.no_loss_check: CLP claim payment, CAS adjustments (CARC/RARC), SVC service-line detail, payment imbalance correction, EOB, automatic secondary billing all preserved.

gaps.feature: Real X12 270/271 eligibility (vs status-flip stub)
gaps.local_evidence: POST /claims/:id/eligibility Zod-validates an empty body and mutates a stored eligibility string; no payer round-trip; billing_claim.eligibility is manual.
gaps.external_evidence: OpenEMR EDI270.php (1162 LoC) builds the 270 inquiry and parses 271 (active coverage, copay, coinsurance, deductible, coverage dates).
gaps.why: Turns the eligibility stub into a real benefits check that also feeds accurate up-front copay estimates the person sees at check-in.
target_files: curaos/backend/services/healthstack-billing-service/src/healthstack-billings/ (wire eligibility route to SDK + payer connector)
steps: 1. Add 270 builder + 271 parser to @curaos/x12 SDK.
2. Add payer-connection config + secrets (clearinghouse endpoint).
3. Replace the eligibility status-flip with a real 270 send / 271 parse; populate copay/deductible.
4. Feed the 271 result into the existing Estimate contract (estimatedCoverageMinor/estimatedCopayMinor) for the check-in copay.
5. Keep manual override for offline/paper payers.
gaps.no_loss_check: 270 inquiry, 271 parse (active coverage, copay, coinsurance, deductible, coverage dates), manual override all preserved.

gaps.feature: Patient coverage / insurance-policy entity (FHIR Coverage, primary/secondary order)
gaps.local_evidence: Only payer string + payerPlanId reference exist; no coverage entity, no subscriber/member-id, no coverage order, no FHIR Coverage in any local module.
gaps.external_evidence: OpenEMR InsuranceService + FhirCoverageService model patient insurance with coverage-type ordering and FHIR Coverage export.
gaps.best_source: openemr (GPL; FHIR Coverage is HL7 published)
gaps.why: Coverage is the shared dependency for eligibility (270/271), claims (837 subscriber/payer loops), and ERA secondary billing - all three gaps need it.
steps: 1. Add FHIR-shaped Coverage contract (subscriber, member-id, payer plan, coverage order, type, dates).
2. Add coverage migration in the healthstack overlay schema (PHI boundary; neutral cores reference-only).
3. Person-facing: patient-app 'my insurance cards' add/update/set-primary.
4. Management: front-office coverage verification + order management; FHIR Coverage export.
gaps.no_loss_check: Policy, payer, subscriber/member-id, coverage order (primary/secondary/tertiary), coverage type/dates, FHIR Coverage all preserved.

gaps.feature: Charge master / fee schedule (priced charge resolution)
gaps.local_evidence: ChargeLine.unitAmountMinor has no authoritative price source; Estimate takes plannedCodes[] but no priced schedule to compute estimatedTotalMinor.
gaps.external_evidence: OpenHospital PricesList/PricesOthers (multi-list, per-item) + OpenEMR fee-schedule resolution.
gaps.why: Without a fee schedule, both estimates and claim charges have no authoritative rate source - estimates and 837 charge amounts cannot be trusted.
steps: 1. Add FeeSchedule contract keyed (tenant, payerPlanId, code, effectiveDate).
2. Migration + admin maintenance CRUD.
3. Resolve ChargeLine.unitAmountMinor + Estimate totals from the schedule.
gaps.no_loss_check: Multiple price lists, per-item override, 'other' charges, effective dating all captured; nothing dropped from OpenHospital.

enrichment.enrichment: Enrich denial_reason free-text into structured CARC/RARC codes + add live AR-aging buckets (0-30/31-60/61-90/90+) and a resubmit/appeal action, fed by claim-lifecycle events.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The local worklist already has a clean projection. Enriching denial codes with plain-language mapping lets the patient-app show 'denied because X, we are appealing' instead of a raw code - the person sees a journey, staff see the CARC/RARC + appeal workflow behind it.

enrichment.local_module: healthstack-billing-service Estimate contract (estimatedCoverageMinor/estimatedCopayMinor already in healthstack-billing.tsp)
enrichment.enrichment: Wire the existing (currently heuristic) estimate to real 271 eligibility output + fee-schedule rates so the copay shown at check-in is authoritative.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The estimate surface is already person-first (patient-app check-in copay). Enriching it with real benefits + real rates makes 'your copay is $X today' trustworthy - a direct quality-of-life win the org-centric sources never offered the patient.

enrichment.local_module: healthstack-billing-service payment capture (idempotent capturePayment, billing_invoice balance write-through)
enrichment.enrichment: Extend the existing payment/balance write-through path to also accept payer-side 835 postings into the same balance, and post the neutral ledger entry via accounting-core-service.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Local already updates the patient-visible balance on cash/card payment. Reusing that exact write-through for insurance payments means the person's 'what I owe' updates automatically when the payer pays - one balance, one truth, no org-side reconciliation the patient never sees.

enrichment.local_module: healthstack-billing-service invoice/charge-line model (integer-minor + fail-closed SafeMinorNonNeg Zod, #369)
enrichment.enrichment: This local money discipline is STRONGER than the GPL sources (OpenHospital uses floats). Reuse it as the canonical money type for the new fee-schedule, remittance, and claim-charge contracts so the X12/835 lanes inherit fail-closed money handling.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Local->source enrichment direction: CuraOS's fail-closed minor-units guard is a correctness upgrade the sources lack; carrying it into every new revenue contract protects the patient from rounding/overcharge errors across the whole cycle.

mappings.feature: General Ledger + double-entry posting
mappings.license_status: GPL/LGPL (port-adapt or service-boundary only; no copy)
mappings.reason: Local GL spine is genuinely strong: balanced journal entries enforced by a DB-level journal_entry_balance_check deferred trigger PLUS an in-code assertBalanced re-assertion, append-only postings, idempotency ledger, audit-chain head hashing, and transactional domain outbox. account_move (Odoo) and bookkeeping (Dolibarr) confirm the model shape but local enforcement (DB trigger + dual assertion) is stronger than source. Mine source only for the policy-rule catalog (which event -> which debit/credit) to widen posting-rules.ts.
mappings.risk: Source uses one mutable account.move row for invoice+bill+entry; copying that anti-pattern would dilute the local append-only ledger. Keep them separate.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: GL is back-office-only by nature; person surface is the derived 'my money' view: a patient/customer sees their own statement (balance, what they owe, what was paid) rendered from their actor-scoped ledger projection, never the raw chart of accounts.
mappings.person_surface: personal-* statement view: my balance, my paid/owed, plain-language line items
mappings.management_surface: accountant trial-balance, journal browser, posting-rule editor, audit-chain verifier - full control retained in accounting admin app
mappings.simplification_note: Source forces manual journal entry UX; CuraOS keeps journals fully automated via the posting-rules engine reacting to domain events, so humans rarely hand-post. No capability lost: manual entry stays available on the management surface.
mappings.no_loss_check: Double-entry, COA, journals, posting, trial-balance building blocks all present locally and enforced; analytic/cost-center tagging filed as gap. No GL capability dropped.
mappings.stronger_than_source_note: DB-trigger balance enforcement exceeds source app-layer-only validation.

mappings.feature: Chart of Accounts management
mappings.reason: accountingAccount table + listAccounts/getAccount/getAccountByCode + POST /accounts + normalSide present. Source value is the seedable COA TEMPLATES (per-country standard charts) which local lacks; mine those as data seeds, not code.
mappings.risk: Low.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: COA is admin-only; no person surface needed beyond friendly account names that flow into the person's statement labels.
mappings.person_surface: none direct (account display names feed statement line labels)
mappings.management_surface: COA CRUD + account-type taxonomy in accounting admin
mappings.simplification_note: Ship pre-seeded standard charts so a tenant never builds a COA from scratch; advanced editing stays available.
mappings.no_loss_check: COA CRUD present; only pre-built localized chart templates are a gap (filed).

mappings.feature: Accounts Receivable (customer invoices + aging)
mappings.reason: AR is event-led locally: sales-core has a 'create draft invoice' op + emits SALES_INVOICE_FINALIZED, accounting-core consumes it and posts AR via posting-rules. But aging buckets, payment terms, dunning, and the receivable balance report are absent. Port ERPNext AR-aging + payment-term logic into the sales contract + a new aging report.
mappings.risk: Splitting invoice ownership (sales=AR origination, accounting=GL posting) must stay clean; do not let accounting own invoice lifecycle.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source AR is org dunning the customer. Re-center: customer sees 'what I owe + due date + pay now' in their personal app; the AR aging/dunning workflow runs as a consequence on the management side.
mappings.person_surface: customer 'pay my invoices' + due-date reminders in personal-commerce/portal app
mappings.management_surface: AR aging report, payment-term config, dunning escalation queue in accounting/sales admin
mappings.simplification_note: Replace dunning email chains with journey-driven nudges in the customer app; the full dunning ladder remains as a management automation.
mappings.no_loss_check: Invoicing + GL posting present; aging, payment terms, dunning are gaps (filed forward). No AR capability silently dropped.

mappings.feature: Accounts Payable (supplier invoices + 3-way match)
mappings.reason: procurement-core already models PR -> PO -> receipt -> supplier invoice (invoicedMinor) -> 3-way match with tolerance band, and emits PROCUREMENT_INVOICE_MATCHED which accounting-core consumes to post AP. This matches/exceeds source for the match flow. Gaps: AP aging, supplier ledger, recurring supplier invoices. Port those.
mappings.risk: Low; the seam already exists and works.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: AP is vendor-org-centric. The 'person' here on the buyer side is the requester/approver: re-center on 'my requests + my approvals' rather than a clerk's invoice queue.
mappings.person_surface: requester app: 'my purchase requests', approver: 'awaiting my approval' inbox
mappings.management_surface: AP aging, supplier ledger, 3-way match exceptions queue, tiered approval matrix in procurement admin
mappings.simplification_note: Auto-match within tolerance posts AP with zero human touch; only exceptions surface to a human, replacing source's manual match screens.
mappings.no_loss_check: PO/receipt/invoice/3-way-match + GL posting present; AP aging, supplier ledger, recurring supplier invoices are gaps (filed). No AP capability dropped.

mappings.feature: Customer invoicing + recurring invoices + PDF
mappings.reason: Only a draft-invoice op exists in sales.tsp; full invoice lifecycle (line items, state machine draft->posted->paid, recurring templates, PDF render, email send) is absent. Dolibarr Facture (6580 LOC) + FactureRec are the most complete reference for the entity model + recurring-template scheduling. Port the model/state-machine; PDF/email goes through existing storage + notify SDKs.
mappings.risk: Invoice numbering must be gap-free + per-tenant + per-fiscal-year (legal requirement); design the sequence carefully.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source invoice card is a 310KB admin form. Person surface: a clean 'your invoice' with one-tap pay; recurring becomes a transparent 'your subscription/plan' the customer can see and manage.
mappings.person_surface: customer invoice view + pay + subscription/recurring self-view in personal app
mappings.management_surface: invoice builder, recurring-template scheduler, numbering config, PDF/email controls in sales/billing admin
mappings.simplification_note: Recurring invoices auto-generate and auto-deliver via notify-sdk; finance only reviews exceptions. Full manual invoice editing retained on the management surface.
mappings.no_loss_check: Currently stub; gap filed with full line-item/state-machine/recurring/PDF scope so nothing from source is lost on build.

mappings.feature: Payment processing (customer + supplier) + payment terms
mappings.reason: commerce-core has gateway payment (Stripe/PayPal bridges) for order checkout; accounting-core has a payouts surface (outbound). Missing: standalone payment-entry against an invoice, partial payments, payment allocation/matching to invoices, supplier payments, payment methods catalog. Port Odoo account.payment allocation + Dolibarr paiement/paiementfourn model.
mappings.risk: Payment-to-invoice allocation must reconcile exactly to GL; partial-payment rounding is a money path - needs a runnable balance check.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Person pays from their own 'pay now' on an invoice; payment method vault is theirs. Source's clerk-recorded payment becomes a self-service action plus an admin manual-record fallback.
mappings.person_surface: customer pay-now + saved payment methods in personal app
mappings.management_surface: manual payment recording, allocation to invoices, supplier payment runs, payment method config in finance admin
mappings.simplification_note: Auto-allocate payments to oldest-open invoice by default; manual allocation stays available. Gateway payments auto-post GL via event.
mappings.no_loss_check: Gateway payment + payouts present; payment-entry/allocation/partial/supplier-payment are gaps (filed). No payment capability dropped.

mappings.feature: Tax management (rules, templates, withholding, computation)
mappings.reason: Only a flat 'tax' field on commerce.carts exists. No tax-rule engine, tax templates, tax categories, withholding, or compound/multi-jurisdiction tax. Odoo account_tax.py (very large, comprehensive incl. tax repartition lines) + ERPNext taxes_and_totals.py are the canonical references. Port a tax-rule engine as a shared package consumed by sales/commerce/procurement at line-compute time.

enrichment.enrichment: Widen the posting-rule catalog from the source debit/credit conventions. Source ERP systems encode the canonical GL effect of every business event (invoice -> AR debit/revenue credit, payment -> cash debit/AR credit, supplier invoice -> expense debit/AP credit). Mine these standard rules to expand DEFAULT_POSTING_RULES so the event-led auto-posting covers the full AR/AP/cash lifecycle, not just the current few triggers.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: More complete auto-posting means the person never sees accounting at all - their pay/invoice action just works and the books stay correct as a silent consequence.

enrichment.enrichment: Seed standard localized chart-of-accounts templates as demo/fixture data. Source ships per-country standard charts; CuraOS currently requires building a COA from scratch. Add pre-seeded charts via service-owned seeds (gen:service-seed) so a new tenant starts with a working COA.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Pre-seeded COA means a small-business/personal user gets meaningful, plain-language account names on their statement immediately, no accountant setup.

enrichment.enrichment: Add tolerance-band + supplier-scorecard + recurring supplier invoices on top of the already-strong 3-way match. Source AP adds recurring supplier invoices (Dolibarr FournisseurFactureRec) and supplier ledgers; layer these onto the existing match engine.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Requester/approver only ever sees 'my requests' and 'awaiting my approval'; recurring vendor bills auto-clear within tolerance so approvers are bothered only by exceptions.

enrichment.enrichment: Local already has audit-chain head hashing (stronger than source plain audit logs). Enrich the REPORTING side using ERPNext's 52 accounting reports (balance sheet, P&L, trial balance, consolidated) as a report-catalog reference to build out financial statements on top of the trustworthy ledger.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The person's 'my money' statement is derived from the same audited ledger that powers the accountant's balance sheet - one source of truth, two re-centered views, both provably tamper-evident.

mappings.feature: Contract / service-agreement management (header + line items, lifecycle draft->validated->closed, attached products/services, contract events agenda)

Person surface = 'My agreements' timeline (what I signed, what it covers, when it renews, what I owe, one-tap view/download). Org process (validate/close lines, renewals) is a consequence of serving that agreement.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app / personal-* 'My Agreements' card list: each agreement shows plain-language coverage, next charge date, signed status, downloadable signed PDF.
mappings.management_surface: business-* / admin contract console: full header+line CRUD, draft->validated->closed lifecycle per line, product/service attach, contract events/agenda, status reporting. No capability dropped vs dolibarr contrat.

mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: header (ref/party/date), line items (product/qty/price/tax), per-line open/closed lifecycle, contract events agenda, signed_status linkage, REST CRUD parity with api_contracts.class.php.
mappings.risk: No local owner at all; net-new service. Must enter generator-first or it will be hand-written and drift.
mappings.reason: Contracts are the spine of this domain and have zero local owner. Dolibarr contrat is the most complete permissive-ish reference (full lifecycle + lines + signing). High reuse of the MODEL via clean-room port.

mappings.feature: Subscriptions & recurring billing (subscription plans, billing interval/count, trial period, billing-period rollover, auto invoice generation, days-until-due, cancel-at-period-end, billing heatmap/history)

mappings.person_centric_reshape: ERPNext Subscription is an accounting back-office doctype (party_type/party, cost_center, accounting dimensions, submit_invoice). Re-center on the person as subscriber: 'My subscriptions' = what I'm paying for, when the next charge lands, what changes at renewal, pause/cancel in one tap. Invoicing + accounting dimensions move to the silent management layer.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop / patient-app 'My Subscriptions': plan name, price, next billing date, trial countdown, manage (pause/cancel-at-period-end) self-serve.
mappings.management_surface: business-shop / admin subscription console: plan catalog (plan/plan_detail), billing interval+count, trial windows, current/next billing period, generate-invoice-at, days-until-due, generate-past-due, billing history/heatmap, additional discount, tax templates.

One scheduler primitive serves donation + subscription + contract renewal.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: plan/plan-detail catalog, billing interval+count, trial start/end, billing-period rollover, auto-invoice generation hook, days-until-due, cancel-at-period-end, additional discount, tax/cost-center dimensions (kept in accounting-core link). mappings.feature: Generic recurring-document / auto-repeat engine (frequency Daily/Weekly/Monthly/Yearly, next_schedule_date, reference any document type, Active/Disabled/Completed status) - the cadence primitive behind subscriptions, renewals, recurring invoices
mappings.license_status: GPL (frappe AutoRepeat) + AGPL (windmill schedule). Reference-grade pattern only; local recurrence.ts is already a cleaner equivalent. No code lifted.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: AutoRepeat/windmill schedule are pure infra (cron rows). Person never sees the scheduler; they see 'next charge / next renewal' as a friendly date. The engine is a shared backend primitive.
mappings.person_surface: Indirect: surfaces as 'next due' date wherever recurring (donations, subscriptions, contract renewals) appears in personal apps.
mappings.management_surface: Admin: schedule status (Active/Disabled/Completed), next_schedule_date, frequency, pause/resume, manual run-now, missed-run backfill.
mappings.simplification_note: Frappe ties AutoRepeat to a global scheduler + DynamicLink reference doctype; CuraOS already does this better with frequency-enum + computed next_due_at + BullMQ delayed job (no cron strings in code). Generalize donation recurrence into a reusable codegen template/SDK helper so every recurring domain inherits the same edge-case-correct cadence (month-end clamp).
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: arbitrary frequency, next-run computation, status lifecycle (active/disabled/completed), reference-any-entity (via generic ownerType+ownerId), missed-run handling. donation recurrence.ts already covers month-end edge cases AutoRepeat handles.
mappings.risk: Generalizing without over-abstracting (ponytail). Keep it a small shared helper, not a framework.
mappings.reason: The cadence primitive is the highest-leverage reusable piece: it unifies donation recurring, subscription billing, and contract renewals. Local donation-core already has the strongest version; promote it to a shared generator template.

mappings.feature: Renewals & renewal alerts (contract/policy term end, auto-renew vs notify, renewal reminders, term rollover)

mappings.reason: Renewals are an event/notify composition over the contract + cadence primitives, not a standalone capability. notify-service already real-working; only the renewal event + template + contract owner are missing.

enrichment.enrichment: Add a public self-serve signing-link surface and a compact denormalized signed_status enum projection on signable subjects, harvested from Dolibarr's online-signature UX. esign-core already exceeds Dolibarr on the engine (eIDAS/UETA, multi-party, chain-of-custody); what Dolibarr adds is (a) a public token signing page for non-account counterparties (newonlinesign.php + onlineSign.php) and (b) a 5-state signed_status enum (NoSignature/SignedSender/SignedReceiver/SignedReceiverOnline/SignedAll) usable as a one-glance summary on the parent agreement.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The person signs their own agreement/renewal in-app via esign-core ops, and can send a self-serve link to a counterparty without forcing them to create an account - the signing journey lives on the person's agreement timeline, not in an org portal. The signed_status enum gives the person a single plain-language 'fully signed / awaiting other party' badge.

enrichment.enrichment: Promote donation-core's recurrence engine (recurrence.ts) from a donation-private helper into a shared cadence primitive consumed by the new subscription-core and contract-core renewals. The source ERPNext Subscription/AutoRepeat models confirm the FIELD SET a complete recurring system needs (trial windows, billing-period rollover, days-until-due, cancel-at-period-end, status lifecycle), which can extend the local enum-based cadence without adopting ERPNext's cron/accounting coupling.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The same correct, edge-case-safe 'next due date' the donor sees for recurring giving becomes the 'next charge' / 'next renewal' a subscriber and an agreement-holder see - one trustworthy cadence the person can rely on across every recurring commitment they hold.

enrichment.enrichment: commerce-core embeds Medusa v2 with Stripe/PayPal bridges but no subscription model; enrich by binding the new subscription-core billing_due event to the existing payment bridge (charge-on-file), informed by Odoo's payment_token (recurring charge token) pattern - so recurring charges reuse the proven checkout/payment plane instead of a parallel one.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The person's saved payment method (already in commerce-core checkout) is reused for every recurring charge across subscriptions, recurring donations, and contract installments - one wallet, one consent, no re-entering card details per recurring commitment.

mappings.feature: Sales Order Management (quote -> order -> confirm; order lines, delivery/invoice status)
mappings.license_status: Odoo=LGPL-3 (sale module), ERPNext=GPL-3. GPL forbids copy-verbatim; port the state machine + field semantics as original TS, no source lift.
mappings.reason: Local sales-core already models the quote->order->invoice->revenue spine in the .tsp + store (createQuote/acceptQuote/createInvoice/recognizeRevenue with line items + commissions). What is weak: the durable drizzle table is still the generic mold table (sales(payload text)) with the real flow living in an InMemory map fallback. Port Odoo/ERPNext order-line + status-transition semantics into real drizzle tables driven from the contract.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source sales_order is a salesperson back-office grid. Re-center on the customer: a personal-shop 'My Quotes & Orders' journey (request a quote, see priced lines, accept, track delivery) is the spine; the salesperson confirm/invoice steps attach to that customer thread.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop app: customer quote-request + accept + order tracking (curaos/frontend/apps/personal-shop)
mappings.management_surface: business-shop app + sales-core: full order pipeline, line editing, commission rules, invoice finalize, revenue recognition (curaos/frontend/apps/business-shop, sales-core-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source needs explicit quote->order->delivery->invoice document chain clicks. CuraOS auto-opens the order on quote-accept (acceptQuote already emits sales.order.created) and derives invoice from the order, so the customer sees one continuous thread while every document still exists for audit.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: quote lifecycle, order lines referencing catalog SKUs, status state machine, invoicing, revenue recognition cap, commission rules. None dropped; persistence hardening filed as gap.
mappings.risk: InMemory fallback masks missing durable order/quote tables; port must land real drizzle tables + migration before live use.

mappings.feature: Purchase Order Management (PO, supplier orders, buying controller, requisition)
mappings.license_status: Dolibarr=GPL-3, Odoo purchase=LGPL-3, ERPNext=GPL-3. Port field/transition semantics only; no verbatim copy of GPL classes.
mappings.reason: procurement-core already has real drizzle tables (procurement_requisition / procurement_order / procurement_receipt / procurement_invoice / procurement_budget) and a PR->PO->receipt->3-way-match->budget flow in the .tsp. Source value is edge-case semantics (partial receipts, multi-line POs, supplier-vs-customer order split per Dolibarr fournisseur.commande) to enrich, not rebuild.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source PO screens are buyer-clerk forms. Re-center on the requester: an employee/clinician 'request to buy' journey (need an item -> requisition -> see approval + ETA) is the spine; buyer PO issuance + matching attach behind it.
mappings.person_surface: requester self-service requisition + status (procurement view in business admin / a personal procurement card)
mappings.management_surface: procurement-core: PO approval, 3-way match, budget projection, supplier orders (procurement-core-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source separates requisition, RFQ, PO, receipt, invoice as distinct manual documents. CuraOS keeps all five as records but drives them from one approval action (approveRequisition mints the PO) so the requester experiences one flow.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: requisition, PO, goods receipt, supplier invoice, 3-way match, budget control. Multi-line/partial-receipt + supplier-order split filed as enrichment.
mappings.risk: Low - local owner is strong; risk is only in matching partial-receipt edge cases against source tolerance bands.

mappings.feature: Procurement / Supplier (vendor) Management + RFQ
mappings.license_status: OpenHospital=GPL-3 (reuse_signal null in index), Odoo purchase_requisition=LGPL-3. Reference only.
mappings.reason: Vendor master + RFQ already named as procurement-core key features (vendor mgmt, RFQ, PO approval, 3-way match) and the schema backs it. Source supplier model is a thin reference for vendor fields (contact, tax id, terms); no new entity needed beyond enriching the vendor record.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Vendors are counterparties, not the person. Person-centric angle is indirect: the requester sees 'who will fulfill this' (preferred supplier + ETA) rather than picking suppliers themselves.
mappings.person_surface: requester sees fulfilling supplier + ETA on their requisition card
mappings.management_surface: procurement-core: vendor master, RFQ, supplier scoring (procurement-core-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source keeps a separate RFQ-to-PO conversion document. CuraOS treats RFQ as a state of the requisition so the buyer does not re-key lines.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: supplier master, RFQ, supplier-PO linkage. Nothing lost.
mappings.risk: Low.

mappings.feature: Quotation / Proposal Management (quote doc, validity, convert-to-order)
mappings.license_status: ERPNext=GPL-3. Port quote-field + validity + convert semantics; no copy.
mappings.reason: sales.tsp already has createQuote/acceptQuote/rejectQuote with priced line items and a QuoteRecord status machine. ERPNext quotation adds validity-until, quote-to-order conversion tracking, and revision fields worth porting into the contract.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source quotation is a sales-rep document. Re-center: the customer requests + receives + accepts a quote in their own app thread; rep authoring is the management mirror.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop 'My Quotes' (request, view priced lines, accept/reject)
mappings.management_surface: business-shop / sales-core quote authoring, pricing, validity, conversion (sales-core-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source quote and sales order are two documents the rep links manually. CuraOS auto-converts on accept (acceptQuote -> OrderResult), removing the re-key while keeping both records.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: quote lines, status, accept/reject, conversion to order. Validity-window + revision filed as enrichment.
mappings.risk: Same durable-persistence gap as sales orders (shared store).

mappings.feature: Pricing Rules & Discounts (conditional pricing, qty breaks, customer-group price)
mappings.license_status: ERPNext=GPL-3. pricing_rule.py logic (apply_pricing_rule, qty/amount conditions) is the most reusable spec; port the rule-evaluation algorithm as original TS, no copy.
mappings.reason: No first-class pricing-rule entity exists locally - commerce-core drizzle has only base+audit tables and sales lines carry a static unit price. ERPNext pricing_rule is the canonical conditional-pricing engine (qty breaks, validity, customer/group/territory conditions, margin rules) and maps cleanly onto a commerce-core price-rule resource feeding sales-core line pricing.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source pricing rules are an admin pricing matrix. Re-center: the customer simply sees the right price + any applied discount with a clear 'why' (loyalty/qty), never the rule grid.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop catalog/cart shows final price + applied-discount badge
mappings.management_surface: business-shop pricing-rule admin: conditions, validity, qty/amount bands, customer-group targeting (commerce-core-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source exposes a complex rule-priority UI. CuraOS keeps full rule expressiveness in the contract but resolves to a single 'best price + reason' the customer and the line item consume.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved when implemented: conditional pricing, qty/amount breaks, validity windows, customer/group/territory scope, discount-vs-rate. Currently a GAP (see gaps).
mappings.risk: Rule-priority + overlap resolution is subtle; must mirror ERPNext priority semantics to avoid wrong prices.

mappings.feature: Product & Service Catalog (products, services, variants, units, batch/lot link)
mappings.license_status: Dolibarr=GPL-3, ERPNext=GPL-3. Catalog already provided by Medusa v2 (MIT) in commerce-core; port only variant/UoM semantics if missing, no GPL copy.
mappings.reason: commerce-core runs Medusa v2 catalog (catalog/cart/checkout, order state machine) per inventory summary, and business-shop.tsp has full Product CRUD + ProductList. Source value is variant-generation (item_variant.py attribute combinations) and service-vs-stockable typing + UoM conversion to enrich the existing catalog.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source item form is a back-office card. Re-center: customer browses a clean catalog (personal-shop) with variants as simple choices; the management catalog editor is the mirror.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop catalog browse/search (curaos/frontend/apps/personal-shop)
mappings.management_surface: business-shop product CRUD, variants, pricing, inventory link (curaos/frontend/apps/business-shop, business-shop-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source exposes 100+ item fields. CuraOS shows the customer name/variant/price/availability and keeps the full field set on the management editor.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: products, services, variants, UoM, batch/lot linkage. Variant-matrix generator + UoM-conversion filed as enrichment.
mappings.risk: Variant-attribute combinatorics can explode; cap with on-demand variant creation like ERPNext.

mappings.feature: Inventory & Stock Control (stock levels, ledger movements, quants, reservations)
mappings.license_status: Odoo stock=LGPL-3, ERPNext=GPL-3, Dolibarr=GPL-3. Local already event-sourced; reference source only for reservation/valuation algorithms.
mappings.reason: inventory-core is genuinely strong: real drizzle tables (inventory_sku, inventory_warehouse, inventory_lot, stock_movement immutable ledger) + receive/issue/adjust/transfer ops + domain events + PG integration tests. This matches Odoo stock_move/stock_quant semantics. Source value is now only reservation (stock_quant reserved_quantity) and valuation, which are absent locally.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Stock is org-internal, but person angle = availability promise: the customer sees real-time 'in stock / X left / restock date' derived from the ledger, never the warehouse grid.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop availability badge sourced from inventory-core levels
mappings.management_surface: inventory-core: warehouse stock, ledger, lot/expiry, movements; business-shop inventory dashboard (inventory-core-service, business-shop-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source needs explicit stock-entry documents per movement. CuraOS exposes typed movement ops (receive/issue/adjust/transfer) that each book one ledger delta, so the operator does one action per real event.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved: stock levels, movement ledger, lot/expiry, transfers, multi-warehouse. Reservations + valuation filed as gap.
mappings.risk: Low for existing scope; reservation race-conditions are the open risk when added.

mappings.feature: Warehouse / Delivery & Fulfillment (delivery note, pick list, picking)
mappings.license_status: ERPNext=GPL-3, Odoo=LGPL-3. Port pick/pack/ship state semantics; no copy.
mappings.reason: business-shop.tsp has fulfillOrder + order status, and inventory-core has transfers, but there is no pick-list / delivery-note / packing entity. Odoo stock_picking + ERPNext pick_list/delivery_note are the canonical pick-pack-ship state machine to port - and it composes with fleet-core for the last-mile leg.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source picking is a warehouse-worker queue. Re-center on the customer: 'preparing -> packed -> shipped -> out for delivery' tracking on their order; the warehouse pick queue is the management mirror feeding it.
mappings.person_surface: personal-shop order tracking timeline (curaos/frontend/apps/personal-shop)
mappings.management_surface: business-shop fulfillment + pick/pack queue; fleet-core last-mile dispatch (business-shop-service, fleet-core-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source has separate delivery-note + pick-list documents. CuraOS models them as fulfillment states on the order with a derived pick list, so the customer sees one tracking thread.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved when built: pick list, packing, delivery note, ship confirmation, stock deduction on ship. Currently partial (no pick/delivery entity) - see gaps.
mappings.risk: Stock deduction must be transactional with ship-confirm against inventory ledger.

mappings.feature: Manufacturing & Production (work order, production order, BOM consumption)
mappings.reason: No local manufacturing module and no person-centric or healthstack driver for shop-floor production orders. Discrete manufacturing (work orders, production planning, shop-floor routing) is out of scope for a person-centric care/commerce platform. Reject for v1; revisit only if a vertical (e.g. compounding pharmacy production) names it.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: No person spine - production is purely org-internal shop-floor work.
mappings.person_surface: none
mappings.management_surface: none (deferred)
mappings.simplification_note: Skip entirely; YAGNI for a person-centric platform.
mappings.no_loss_check: Feature intentionally not built; filed forward, not lost. If a compounding/lab-prep vertical needs it, BOM+work-order enter via a healthstack-overlay contract then.
mappings.risk: Building MRP now is large speculative scope with no consumer.

mappings.feature: Bill of Materials (BOM) (components, qty, multi-level explosion, operations)
mappings.reason: BOM only exists to drive manufacturing/MRP, which is rejected for v1 (no consumer). A kit/bundle product (catalog feature) covers the realistic person-centric need (a care kit = N items) without a multi-level BOM engine; that belongs in catalog, not a BOM module.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: If a 'kit' is ever needed, the customer just sees one bundle product; no BOM exposure.
mappings.person_surface: none (or a future catalog bundle/kit)
mappings.management_surface: none for v1; catalog kit if/when needed
mappings.simplification_note: Replace the entire BOM concept with a simple catalog bundle/kit if the need appears; do not port the multi-level explosion engine.
mappings.no_loss_check: Manufacturing-BOM intentionally deferred with MRP. Kit/bundle need re-routed to catalog enrichment, not lost.
mappings.risk: Speculative; no consumer.

mappings.feature: Point of Sale (POS sessions, POS invoice, closing entry, offline order)
mappings.license_status: ERPNext=GPL-3, Dolibarr TakePOS=GPL-3. Reference the POS-session + closing-entry data model only; no copy.
mappings.reason: No local POS, but checkout already exists in commerce-core (cart/checkout/order state machine) and business-shop. POS is mostly a counter-staff UI over the same order + payment + inventory primitives plus a cash-session (open/close/reconcile) concept. Reference ERPNext pos_closing_entry for the session-reconcile model; build POS as a thin app surface over existing services, not a new domain service.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: In-store POS still centers the customer: a fast counter checkout + the same customer order/receipt thread (digital receipt to their app), not a separate POS silo.
mappings.person_surface: customer digital receipt + order history (personal-shop)
mappings.management_surface: POS app: cashier checkout, cash drawer session open/close, reconciliation, offline queue (a future business app over commerce-core + business-shop-service)
mappings.simplification_note: Source POS duplicates the sales doc chain. CuraOS POS reuses the commerce checkout + order so an in-store sale and an online sale are the same order, with only a cash-session wrapper added.
mappings.no_loss_check: Preserved when built: POS order, tender/payment, cash session open/close, reconciliation, offline. Currently absent - filed forward (lower priority than pricing/fulfillment).
mappings.risk: Offline-first POS + cash reconciliation is the real complexity; defer until a retail consumer needs it.

gaps.feature: Conditional pricing engine (pricing rules, qty breaks, customer-group pricing, validity windows)
gaps.local_evidence: commerce-core drizzle/schema.ts has only base + audit/outbox tables (no price-rule entity); sales-core line items carry a static unit price (sales-store.ts LineItemRecord). No first-class discount/pricing-rule resource anywhere.
gaps.external_evidence: ERPNext pricing_rule is a complete engine: external-sources/erp-business/erpnext/erpnext/accounts/doctype/pricing_rule/pricing_rule.py (21.8K) + utils.py (23.4K apply_pricing_rule) cover conditions (item/group/territory/customer-group), qty/amount bands, validity dates, discount-pct vs rate vs margin, and rule priority.
gaps.why: Pricing rules are the highest-leverage missing trade feature - they affect every quote, cart, and POS line. Person-centric value: the customer always sees the correct discounted price with a clear reason. Management value: full promotional/contract-pricing control.
steps: Add PriceRule + PriceResult models to commerce.tsp (conditions, qty/amount bands, validity, scope, discount type) - contract first per generator-evolution
Compile .tsp -> OpenAPI -> regenerate commerce SDK; add drizzle price_rule table via migration
Port ERPNext apply_pricing_rule resolution (priority + overlap) as original TS in commerce-core service
Have sales-core line pricing + commerce cart call commerce-core price resolution instead of static unit price
Surface 'applied discount + reason' on personal-shop cart and full rule editor on business-shop
Add rule-priority/overlap test mirroring ERPNext test_pricing_rule cases
gaps.no_loss_check: Must preserve conditional scope (item/group/territory/customer-group), qty/amount breaks, validity windows, discount-pct vs flat-rate vs margin, and deterministic priority resolution - no source pricing capability dropped.

gaps.feature: Order fulfillment chain (pick list, delivery note, pack/ship states, stock deduction on ship)
gaps.local_evidence: business-shop.tsp has fulfillOrder + order status but no pick-list/delivery-note entity; inventory-core has transfers but no ship-confirm that deducts stock against an order line.
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo stock_picking (addons/stock/models/stock_picking.py) + ERPNext pick_list.json + delivery_note.json model the full pick->pack->ship state machine with backorders and per-line stock moves.
gaps.why: Closes the gap between an accepted order and stock leaving the warehouse, and gives the customer a real tracking timeline. Composes with fleet-core for last-mile.
steps: Add fulfillment states (preparing/packed/shipped/delivered) + a derived pick list to shop.tsp - contract first
Add a ship-confirm op in inventory.tsp that books an issue ledger delta per order line atomically
Regenerate shop + inventory SDKs from compiled OpenAPI
Wire ship-confirm to emit a domain event consumed by fleet-core for last-mile dispatch
Render the customer tracking timeline in personal-shop; pick/pack queue in business-shop
gaps.no_loss_check: Preserve pick list, packing, delivery note, partial shipment/backorder, and transactional stock deduction on ship - no source fulfillment capability dropped.

gaps.feature: Durable sales quote/order/invoice persistence (replace InMemory map fallback)
gaps.local_evidence: sales-core-service/drizzle/schema.ts base table is the generic mold sales(payload text); the rich quote/order/invoice/commission flow lives in an InMemoryStore (sales-store.ts private Map quotes/orders) with the Postgres-backed store path not yet the durable default.
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo sale_order.py + ERPNext sales_order.py persist orders, lines, and status transitions in real tables with delivery/invoice status fields - the durable model CuraOS must match.
gaps.why: The sales domain logic is correct but not durably persisted; without real tables the quote->order->invoice flow cannot survive restart or scale, blocking live use.
steps: Add sales_quote / sales_quote_line / sales_order / sales_invoice / commission_rule drizzle tables modeled on the .tsp models + a migration
Make the Postgres store the default SalesStore (keep InMemory for unit tests only per demo-data rule)
Add a service-owned seed (gen:service-seed) so demo data is database-backed, not API-mocked
Run PG integration tests proving quote->accept->order->invoice->revenue persists across restart
gaps.no_loss_check: Preserve every existing domain capability (quote lifecycle, line items, order handoff, invoice finalize, revenue-recognition cap, commission rules) - this hardens persistence, removes nothing.

gaps.feature: Stock reservation + inventory valuation (reserved-qty, available-to-promise, moving-avg/FIFO cost)
gaps.local_evidence: inventory-core has a strong movement ledger (stock_movement) + levels but no reserved-quantity concept and no cost/valuation field; availability is on-hand only.
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo stock_quant.py tracks reserved_quantity (available = on-hand - reserved); ERPNext stock_controller.py + valuation track moving-average/FIFO cost per stock entry.
gaps.why: Reservation gives the customer an accurate available-to-promise (no overselling); valuation gives management cost-of-goods reporting. Both are required for correct order fulfillment + margin pricing.
steps: Add reserve/unreserve ops + a reservation ledger entry type to inventory.tsp - contract first
Add valuation fields (unit_cost, valuation_method) and a cost-layer table for FIFO/moving-avg via migration
Regenerate inventory SDK; emit reservation + valuation domain events
Expose available-to-promise (on-hand - reserved) to personal-shop availability badge and to the order/fulfillment path
gaps.no_loss_check: Preserve full reservation lifecycle (reserve on order, release on cancel, consume on ship) and both valuation methods - no source stock-accuracy capability dropped.

enrichment.enrichment: Add UDI/GS1-class lot semantics and expiry-driven FEFO (first-expired-first-out) issue logic to the existing lot table, plus expiry-alert domain events.
enrichment.source: dolibarr productbatch + erpnext item batch/serial; openhospital medical inventory
enrichment.person_centric_angle: For HealthStack, expiry-aware lot tracking protects the patient: a person's dispensed medication/device is always traceable to a non-expired lot, and recall events can reach the exact people who received an affected lot. The lot record stays device-id (not PHI) per the existing inventory.tsp note.

enrichment.enrichment: Deepen the catalog with a variant-matrix generator (attribute combinations -> SKUs) and service-vs-stockable typing + unit-of-measure conversion, driven from the contract.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Customer sees variants as simple choices (size/color) in personal-shop with the right price + availability per variant, never the SKU matrix; the management catalog editor owns the full variant/UoM control.

enrichment.enrichment: Add multi-line PO support, partial-receipt handling with tolerance bands, and the supplier-order vs customer-order split semantics to the existing 3-way-match flow.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The requester sees a single 'request to buy' with partial-delivery progress (3 of 5 received) rather than juggling multiple receipt documents; the buyer keeps full multi-line/partial-match control.

enrichment.enrichment: Add quote validity windows + revision tracking and richer commission models (split/tiered) onto the existing quote + commission-rule surface.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The customer sees a clear 'valid until' on their quote and a single accept action; the rep keeps revision history + commission attribution on the management mirror.

enrichment.enrichment: Build POS as a thin counter app over the existing checkout/order primitives, adding only a cash-session (open/close/reconcile) wrapper and offline order queue.
enrichment.source: erpnext pos_invoice + pos_closing_entry + point_of_sale page; dolibarr takepos
enrichment.person_centric_angle: An in-store sale produces the same customer order + digital receipt as an online sale (unified order thread), so the person's purchase history is complete across channels; only the cash-drawer reconciliation is staff-side.

mappings.feature: FHIR R4 resource provider framework (Patient, Observation, Encounter, Condition, AllergyIntolerance, MedicationRequest, DiagnosticReport, Immunization, Practitioner/Role, Location, Task, ServiceRequest)
mappings.license_status: openmrs-fhir2 MPL-2.0 (port-adapt-with-file-notice, file-level copyleft); fhir-on-vista + vista-fhir-codex Apache-2.0 (copy-verbatim-ok-with-NOTICE)
mappings.reason: openmrs-fhir2's ToFhirTranslator<T,U>/FromFhirTranslator<T,U> generic interface pair is the single most reusable interop pattern across the whole corpus: it cleanly separates domain model <-> FHIR resource mapping, the exact seam our generator needs. We do NOT copy the Java (MPL file-copyleft); we port the PATTERN as a TS translator-pair convention emitted by the generator. The per-resource extraction logic (vista-fhir-codex bundle aggregation, fhir-on-vista parser/service layer) is permissive and confirms the resource set + edge cases (FileMan<->FHIR date conversion, ICN lookups, dedup).
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source frameworks expose a flat org-wide FHIR endpoint (clinician/registry pulls any patient). Re-center: the patient-app consumes its OWN compartment (Patient/$everything scoped to the authenticated person via healthstack-consent SMART scopes) as the spine of 'my record'; the clinician/registry surface keeps the full multi-patient query.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app 'My health record' timeline assembled from the person's own FHIR Patient compartment (read), with export-to-provider.
mappings.management_surface: clinician + healthstack admin full FHIR R4 REST server (multi-patient search, all resource types, bulk export) for interoperability with external EHRs/HIEs.
mappings.simplification_note: Generator emits one translator pair + provider per resource from the .tsp contract, so all 13+ resources share one tested mold instead of 13 hand-written Java provider/parser/service triples; new resources are a contract edit, not new code.
mappings.no_loss_check: All source resource types (Patient, Observation, Encounter, Condition, Allergy, Medication[Request|Statement|Dispense], DiagnosticReport/ServiceRequest, Immunization, Practitioner/Role, Location, Task, Group, CareTeam, Procedure) enumerated and mapped to generated translator pairs; patient-compartment + dual R3/R4 versioning preserved as contract config.
mappings.risk: Largest single lane: FHIR conformance is broad. Risk of partial resource coverage. Mitigate by driving every resource from the contract so coverage is checklist-verifiable, and using HAPI/fhir.js validation in CI.

mappings.feature: OpenMRS<->FHIR translator framework (generic bidirectional model<->resource mapping seam)
mappings.license_status: MPL-2.0 (file-level copyleft) -> pattern reference only, no code copy; design our own TS interfaces
mappings.reason: The architectural lesson (a typed translator pair per domain object, reference-translators for cross-resource links, category/code translators that consult terminology-service) becomes a generator emitter convention in @curaos/codegen and a shared abstract base in @curaos/fhir-client. This is the no-loss way to absorb fhir2's design without inheriting MPL obligations on copied files.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: n/a (internal mapping seam) - it serves the patient surface by guaranteeing the person's data round-trips losslessly to/from FHIR.
mappings.person_surface: indirect: powers correct 'my record' export and provider sharing.
mappings.management_surface: indirect: powers conformant FHIR server for external integrators.
mappings.simplification_note: One emitter convention replaces per-service bespoke mapping code; reference translators centralize the link-resolution that fhir2 spreads across dozens of classes.
mappings.no_loss_check: Bidirectional (to+from FHIR), reference resolution, and category/code-to-terminology lookup all captured as emitter responsibilities; nothing dropped.
mappings.risk: Low - pattern only, no license exposure.

mappings.feature: FHIR terminology server: $expand / $lookup / $translate / $validate-code over CodeSystem, ValueSet, ConceptMap (SNOMED CT, LOINC, RxNorm, ICD-10)
mappings.reason: Local terminology-service is real-working and runs Snowstorm (SNOMED/LOINC/RxNorm/ICD-10) with all four FHIR terminology operations ($expand, $lookup, $translate, $validate-code) and CodeSystem/ConceptMap/ValueSet present + a generated terminology-sdk + ValueSetUpdated event. Source terminology support (fhir-on-vista BSTS codeset lookup, vista-fhir-codex codeset services) is weaker and VistA-coupled. Nothing to import; this is a local->source win. See enrichment for the one thing worth taking (value-set binding semantics from pophealth/HDS).
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source terminology is admin-only. Re-center: terminology powers human-readable labels in the patient-app ('your A1c result' not 'LOINC 4548-4'); the management surface keeps raw code browsing/mapping.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app shows plain-language terms resolved server-side from codes.
mappings.management_surface: terminology admin: code system import, value-set authoring, concept-map mapping, $translate console.
mappings.simplification_note: Snowstorm as a managed dependency means we never hand-maintain code tables the way VistA M code does.
mappings.no_loss_check: All source terminology ops are a subset of local; confirmed superset.
mappings.risk: None - reject keeps current strong implementation.

mappings.feature: Clinical Quality Measure (CQM/eCQM) execution engine: population criteria (IPP/DENOM/NUMER/DENEX/DENEXCEP/NUMEX/MSRPOPL/OBSERV/STRAT), measure calculation over a patient cohort
mappings.license_status: pophealth + health-data-standards Apache-2.0 (copy-verbatim-ok-with-NOTICE) -> we port logic to TS but may copy verbatim with NOTICE if Ruby kept as background service
mappings.reason: quality-service is scaffold-only ('HEDIS/CMS eCQM TBD'). pophealth + HDS are the canonical permissive eCQM stack: the population-criteria taxonomy (the 10 ALL_POPULATION_CODES constants) and measure-calculation semantics are the exact business rules we must not lose. Port the population-criteria model + measure-calc algorithm into a TS quality-service driven by a generated contract; the HQMF/QRDA parsing can run as an Apache-2.0 background converter service rather than be reimplemented.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: pophealth is 100% provider/practice population dashboard (org-centric). Re-center: the SAME measure logic produces a patient-facing 'my care gaps' surface ('your overdue colorectal screening', 'A1c due') in patient-app; the management surface keeps the population/practice dashboard.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app 'care gaps & screenings due' card derived from the person's own measure evaluation.
mappings.management_surface: healthstack-quality admin: practice/provider population cohorts, measure performance, baselines/targets, payer reporting.
mappings.simplification_note: One measure-eval engine feeds both the population dashboard and the individual care-gap nudge, instead of a separate clinical-decision-support rebuild; baselines/targets become contract-configured.
mappings.no_loss_check: All 10 population codes, stratification (STRAT), observation (OBSERV/CV measures), measure baselines + performance targets, and cohort/patient-filtering preserved; provider/practice hierarchy preserved as management surface.

mappings.feature: HQMF measure import & parsing (Health Quality Measure Format -> executable measure model)
mappings.license_status: Apache-2.0 (copy-verbatim-ok-with-NOTICE) - safe to run the Ruby HDS lib as-is behind a service boundary with NOTICE
mappings.reason: HQMF parsing is gnarly XML->model work that HDS already does correctly and permissively. Reimplementing in TS is high-risk low-value. Best: wrap the Apache-2.0 HDS hqmf-parser as a background converter invoked by conversion-core-service (which already does PDF/FHIR/HL7/CSV conversion + async job queue), emitting a MeasureImported event consumed by quality-service. No code rewrite; the measure model becomes our contract.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Pure back-office import (no person surface). It feeds the person-facing care-gap measures with correctly-imported definitions.
mappings.person_surface: indirect: correct measures -> accurate 'my care gaps'.
mappings.management_surface: quality admin: upload/import HQMF/eCQM definitions, version measures.
mappings.simplification_note: Reuse the existing conversion-core async-job + converter pattern instead of a new parser; HDS does the hard XML.
mappings.no_loss_check: HQMF document model, data_criteria, population_criteria, precondition trees, generator (round-trip) all available via the wrapped lib; nothing lost.
mappings.risk: Running a Ruby sidecar adds an ops dependency; acceptable for an import-time-only, non-hot-path converter.

mappings.feature: QRDA Category I (per-patient) + Category III (aggregate) export, C32/C-CDA document generation, and QRDA/CDA validation
mappings.reason: clinical-doc-service already has a CDA bridge + FHIR Composition/DocumentReference (real-working) but QRDA I/III is absent. HDS owns mature, permissive QRDA I/III + C32/C-CDA generation AND validators. Run HDS as the export/validate converter (same background-service pattern as HQMF import); quality-service produces the QRDA III aggregate from its measure results, conversion-core emits the document, clinical-doc-service stores it as a DocumentReference.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Source treats QRDA as a regulatory submission artifact. Re-center: QRDA I per-patient export becomes part of the person's 'download/share my quality record'; QRDA III stays a management/payer submission.
mappings.person_surface: patient-app 'export my record' can emit QRDA I (and C-CDA) for the person to take to another provider.
mappings.management_surface: quality admin: generate + validate QRDA III for CMS/payer submission, batch C-CDA exports.
mappings.simplification_note: Validation built into the same converter so malformed submissions fail fast; clinical-doc-service is the one document store for both FHIR and CDA/QRDA outputs.
mappings.no_loss_check: QRDA Cat I, Cat III, C32/HITSP C32, and document validation all preserved via the wrapped HDS lib; clinical-doc CDA bridge retained.
mappings.risk: QRDA schema conformance is strict; rely on HDS validators in CI.

mappings.feature: Value Set management & terminology binding for measures (measure code -> value set -> member codes)
mappings.reason: terminology-service already does ValueSet $expand and emits ValueSetUpdated. What's missing is the MEASURE->value-set binding layer pophealth/HDS provide (a measure references value sets by OID; members resolve via expansion). Port the binding semantics into quality-service, which calls terminology-service $expand via api-adapter and subscribes to ValueSetUpdated to invalidate measure caches.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Binding is internal; it ensures the person's codes are matched correctly to a measure ('your LOINC result counts toward the diabetes measure').
mappings.person_surface: indirect: accurate care-gap matching.
mappings.management_surface: quality admin: see which value sets a measure binds, re-expand on terminology update.
mappings.simplification_note: Reuse existing $expand + ValueSetUpdated event rather than embedding a value-set store in quality-service.
mappings.no_loss_check: OID-based value-set references, expansion-driven membership, and update-invalidation preserved.
mappings.risk: Low - bounded integration against an existing strong service.

mappings.feature: Public-health quality reporting: aggregate measure submission (QRDA III + Excel/CSV extracts), patient filtering / population cohort definition, measure baselines & performance targets
mappings.reason: This is the reporting/submission half of the eCQM story and the public-health-reporting taxonomy leaf. pophealth's record_filter (cohort definition), measure_baseline (targets), and serializers are the permissive reference. Port into quality-service contract + reports surface.
mappings.person_centric_reshape: pophealth is a population dashboard for the practice. Re-center: the person never sees aggregate reporting, but their inclusion is consent-gated; the management surface owns the population view and submission.
mappings.person_surface: n/a directly; the person sees only their own care gaps (derived from the same engine).
mappings.management_surface: quality admin: define cohorts/filters, set baselines + targets, generate QRDA III + Excel/CSV extracts for public-health/payer submission.
mappings.simplification_note: Cohort filter + baseline config become contract-driven; extracts reuse reports-sdk/export rather than a bespoke Excel path.
mappings.no_loss_check: Cohort filtering, baselines, performance targets, QRDA III + Excel/CSV export all preserved.
mappings.risk: Medium - submission formats are regulated; validate against HDS.

mappings.feature: HL7v2 ADT / message ingestion + FileMan<->FHIR datetime + bundle dedup/entity-mapping (legacy EHR integration plumbing)
mappings.reason: interop-service README explicitly targets 'FHIR Server/HL7v2 ADT/C-CDA TBD'. fhir-on-vista's REST layer + vista-fhir-codex's bundle dedup, entity mapping, and FileMan<->FHIR date conversion are permissive, battle-tested edge-case handlers for legacy ingestion. Port the dedup/date/mapping utilities (they're generic) and the HL7v2 ADT ingestion shape; emit normalized FHIR resources as events.

Re-center: ingestion creates/updates the person's record so their patient-app immediately reflects external-system data ('your hospital visit was imported'); management owns the integration config + error queue.
