mappings.management_surface: interop admin: HL7v2/FHIR endpoint config, message error/dead-letter queue, dedup review.
mappings.simplification_note: Dedup + date normalization become shared fhir-client utilities (one tested copy) instead of per-integration code.
mappings.no_loss_check: ADT ingestion, bundle dedup, entity mapping, datetime conversion all preserved as utilities + events.
mappings.risk: HL7v2 has many message types; scope to ADT first, file the rest forward.

gaps.feature: eCQM execution engine (CQM measure calculation over patient cohorts) - the core of healthstack-quality-service
gaps.local_evidence: healthstack-quality-service is scaffold-only: README + Helm chart, zero src; inventory feature 'README only, HEDIS/CMS eCQM TBD'.
gaps.external_evidence: pophealth lib/hds/measure.rb (CQM execution engine) + health-data-standards lib/hqmf-model/population_criteria.rb defines the full population taxonomy (IPP/DENOM/NUMER/DENEX/DENEXCEP/NUMEX/MSRPOPL/OBSERV/MSRPOPLEX/STRAT).
gaps.suggested_mode: port-adapt (TS engine) with HDS Ruby retained as a golden-file reference; HQMF import wrapped as background service
gaps.why: Quality is a v1 healthstack overlay leaf with no implementation; the permissive eCQM stack is the canonical, regulator-aligned reference and must not be reinvented from scratch.
steps: 1. Author healthstack-quality.tsp contract: Measure, PopulationCriteria (10 codes), MeasureResult, Cohort, Baseline entities + ops (generator-first; entity enters via .tsp, not hand-written service).
2. Regenerate service skeleton + @curaos/healthstack-quality-sdk from the contract.
3. Port population-criteria eval + measure-calc from pophealth/HDS into src/measures (TS).
4. Wrap HDS hqmf-parser as an Apache-2.0 background converter behind conversion-core-service; emit MeasureImported event.
5. quality-service subscribes to terminology ValueSetUpdated + calls $expand (api-adapter) for value-set binding.
6. Add golden-file tests comparing TS engine output to pophealth/HDS reference outputs.
gaps.no_loss_check: All 10 population codes, stratification, continuous-variable (OBSERV) measures, baselines/targets, cohort filtering, and QRDA III/Excel reporting are explicitly carried into the contract + tests; provider/practice population dashboard retained as management surface; per-patient care-gap surface added for the person.

gaps.feature: QRDA I/III + C-CDA generation and validation
gaps.local_evidence: clinical-doc-service has a CDA bridge + FHIR Composition/DocumentReference (real-working) but no QRDA; quality-service stub has no submission output.
gaps.external_evidence: health-data-standards QRDA Cat I/III export + C32 generation + qrda_qdm_template_validator + measure_validator.
gaps.suggested_mode: run-as-background-service (wrap HDS export/validate) invoked by conversion-core-service
gaps.why: Submission + validation are regulated and already solved permissively; reimplementation is high-risk, low-value.
steps: 1. Add QRDA/C-CDA convert + validate jobs to conversion-core (reuse async-job queue).
2. quality-service emits MeasureResultsReady -> conversion-core generates QRDA III.
3. clinical-doc-service stores QRDA I per-patient + QRDA III aggregate as DocumentReference.
4. Wire HDS validators into CI for both.
gaps.no_loss_check: QRDA I, QRDA III, C32/C-CDA, and validation all preserved; person-facing QRDA I export added; management QRDA III submission retained.

gaps.feature: FHIR R4 server (full resource provider set) + HL7v2 ADT ingestion + C-CDA exchange
gaps.local_evidence: healthstack-interop-service is scaffold-only ('clean slate', README + Helm); fhir-client package is README-only ('clean slate').
gaps.external_evidence: openmrs-fhir2 (13+ R4 providers + translator framework, MPL-2.0 port-adapt), fhir-on-vista + vista-fhir-codex (permissive resource extraction, bundle dedup, FileMan<->FHIR datetime).
gaps.suggested_mode: port-adapt via generator (translator-pair emitter convention in @curaos/codegen + @curaos/fhir-client base)
gaps.why: Interop FHIR server is a v1 healthstack leaf with no code; this is the platform's external-EHR/HIE bridge and the export path for 'my record'.
steps: 1. Define a ToFhir/FromFhir translator-pair convention + reference-translator + datetime/dedup utils in @curaos/fhir-client (pattern-ported from fhir2, code from permissive vista sources).
2. Author healthstack-interop.tsp with the FHIR resource operation set (generator-first).
3. Add a codegen emitter that scaffolds a translator pair + provider per resource referenced in the contract.
4. Regenerate interop-service; implement per-resource mapping to neutral/healthstack domain services.
5. Add HL7v2 ADT ingestion (port-adapt) emitting normalized FHIR resources as events.
6. FHIR-validate every resource in CI.
gaps.no_loss_check: Full R4 resource set, patient compartment, dual R3/R4 versioning, HL7v2 ADT, C-CDA, bundle dedup, and FileMan<->FHIR datetime all carried; person 'my record' export + management multi-patient FHIR server both delivered.

enrichment.enrichment: Add measure-aware value-set binding metadata to ValueSet expansion: when a value set is referenced by an eCQM (by OID), tag the expansion + the ValueSetUpdated event with the binding so quality-service can invalidate measure caches on terminology change. Local already has $expand + ValueSetUpdated; the gap is the measure-binding linkage pophealth/HDS encode.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Correct value-set binding is what lets the patient-app say 'your LOINC 4548-4 result counts toward your diabetes care measure' in plain language - the person sees an accurate, explained care gap, not an opaque code.

enrichment.enrichment: Extend the existing CDA bridge (real-working: FHIR Composition/DocumentReference + IHE MHD) to also produce/consume C32/C-CDA and store QRDA documents as DocumentReference. clinical-doc already owns FHIR Consent + signatures; it is the natural single document store for both FHIR and CDA/QRDA outputs.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The person gets one 'download/share my record' action that emits a C-CDA or QRDA I they can hand to any external provider, consent-gated via the existing healthstack-consent SMART scopes - one document home, person-controlled sharing.

enrichment.enrichment: Add HQMF-import and QRDA/C-CDA-export converter jobs to the existing async job queue (already does PDF/FHIR/HL7/CSV). This lets us run the Apache-2.0 HDS Ruby lib as a background converter behind a stable contract instead of reimplementing XML parsing/generation, and reuses the existing job/queue/dead-letter machinery.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Conversion runs invisibly so the person's import (external hospital data) and export (their portable record) just work, surfaced as simple status in the patient-app rather than the format-juggling the source tools expose to admins.

enrichment.enrichment: Bake the openmrs-fhir2 translator-pair pattern (ToFhirTranslator<T,U>/FromFhirTranslator<T,U> + reference translators) into a codegen emitter convention and a shared base in @curaos/fhir-client, plus permissive utilities (bundle dedup, FileMan<->FHIR datetime) from the Apache-2.0 vista sources. This makes every future FHIR-exposing service generate conformant, tested mapping code from its .tsp contract rather than hand-writing provider/parser/service triples.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Generator-emitted, conformance-tested translators guarantee the person's data round-trips losslessly to/from FHIR, so 'my record' is always complete and correct no matter which service owns the underlying data.

gaps.feature: Policy lifecycle core (policy + carrier-ref + coverage-lines + term/renewal + endorsements + beneficiaries) - the entire insurance policy domain
gaps.local_evidence: No policy/insurance/carrier/premium/endorsement/beneficiary entity in 167-module inventory; crm-core + sales-core are generic CRM/sales partials.
gaps.external_evidence: SuiteCRM AOS_Contracts + AOS_Line_Item_Groups + AOS_Invoices; Dolibarr contrat/contratligne; EspoCRM custom-entity (EntityManager) pattern - all model policy as generic contract+line-items, none ship a true insurance policy module.
gaps.best_source: SuiteCRM AOS_Contracts (data shape) + Dolibarr contrat (lifecycle) - both GPL-3, port-adapt only
gaps.why: Insurance-broker is a NET-NEW domain locally. The policy is the spine all other features hang on (carrier, lines, premium, endorsements, beneficiaries, commission, claims all FK to policy). Must enter generator-first as a new domain.
steps: 1. Author policy domain TypeSpec contract (.tsp): Policy, CoverageLine, PolicyTerm, Endorsement, Beneficiary, Carrier-ref entities + lifecycle state-machine + 100%-beneficiary-sum + premium validators.
2. Run @curaos/codegen 3-layer emit -> policy-core-service (neutral) + business-insurance + personal-insurance overlays + insurance-sdk + Helm + gateway route + ai-mirror docs.
3. Add Drizzle schema + migration + gen:service-seed real demo policies (database-backed per demo-data rule).
4. Wire carrier/beneficiary/producer refs to party-core via composite-FK; reuse line-item template from sales-core.
5. Add AsyncAPI events curaos.business.insurance.policy.bound.v1 / renewed / endorsed / lapsed.
6. Fold all term/pro-rata/sum edge cases into generator validators + snapshot tests.
gaps.no_loss_check: Every AOS_Contracts/contrat field (number, status, dates, parties, line items, totals, lifecycle) preserved as policy fields; insurance-specific fields (carrier, perils, limits, deductibles) added; nothing dropped.

gaps.feature: Premium billing schedules & installments (recurring premium AR, pay plans, delinquency/cancellation-for-nonpay, carrier remittance reconciliation)

gaps.external_evidence: SuiteCRM AOS_Invoices recurring/installment; Dolibarr contract billing; OpenEMR billing AR patterns.
gaps.best_source: SuiteCRM AOS_Invoices + Dolibarr contract billing (GPL-3, port-adapt)

steps: 1. Add PremiumSchedule + Installment entities to insurance .tsp.
2. Generate installment-creation that posts to accounting-core AR (double-entry).
3. Wire local recurrence module (inventory lists 'recurrence' package) for schedule generation.
4. Add delinquency state-machine + cancel-for-nonpay workflow + autopay.
5. Add carrier-remittance reconciliation report on management surface.
6. Database-backed demo installments via gen:service-seed.
gaps.no_loss_check: Frequency, installment amounts, AR posting, late fees, delinquency, cancel-for-nonpay, carrier remittance reconciliation all preserved; person gets simple autopay/pay-now without losing back-office capability.

gaps.feature: Commission engine with splits & multi-level overrides (rule-based commission calc, split %, hierarchy override cascade, clawback on cancellation, carrier-statement reconciliation, producer payout runs)

gaps.feature: Self-serve ad-hoc report/query builder (user-defined fields, group-by, filters, charts, saved + scheduled report definitions)
gaps.local_evidence: reports-service has audit/auth/commerce/db/events/ops/providers/reports dirs but grep for cohort|adhoc|query.?builder|pivot|chart returns NOTHING; key_features = 'Gotenberg PDF; scheduled jobs; Superset dashboards' (delivery + embed only, no first-party definition engine).
gaps.external_evidence: SuiteCRM AOR_Report.php defines a full report-definition model (fields, group-by, filters, chart types) + AOR_Scheduled_Reports + AOR_Charts; Frappe report.py + query_report.py provide query-report definitions; pophealth provides cohort/patient-filtering as report inputs.

gaps.why: Local can render and schedule PDFs and embed Superset, but a person/admin cannot define their own ad-hoc report (pick fields, group, filter, chart) as a first-party saved object. This is the largest concrete reporting gap and is required for the management surface.
steps: Define a ReportDefinition entity in @curaos/contracts (reports .tsp): dataset/source entity ref, selected fields (from contract entity catalog), group-by, filter conditions, chart spec, schedule (reuse existing scheduled-jobs).
Regenerate reports-sdk from the updated TypeSpec (zero hand-written transport).
Implement ReportDefinition CRUD + run endpoint in reports-service/src/reports, executing against the field catalog and feeding Gotenberg/Superset for output.
Drive the field catalog from contract entity definitions (closes generator gap: builder lists real typed fields automatically).
Add gen:service-seed sample report definitions (DB-backed demo data, not API mocks).
Expose person-scoped (my-data) and admin-scoped (full) variants via the same definition + RBAC scope.
gaps.no_loss_check: Field select, group-by, filter conditions, chart types, scheduled delivery (already local) all preserved as a typed contract entity; no SuiteCRM AOR capability dropped, definition is interoperable via contract not PHP.

Seed default per-role workspace templates (DB-backed).
gaps.no_loss_check: ehmp predefined+user screens, applet manifest, saved-per-user config all map to surface-type + SavedLayout; clinician richer workspace = management surface, person board = person surface; nothing dropped.

Approval/billing is a downstream consequence.
mappings.person_surface: personal-tasks / personal-hr: per-task timer, my-week summary, submit-for-approval; person owns their logged hours.
mappings.management_surface: hr-core + business-projects: approval workflow, costing rate, billable hours, billing rollup to sales/accounting, utilization reports.
mappings.simplification_note: hr-core already has TimeEntry + recordTimeEntry. Extend (not fork) with task/project ref + billable flag + rate; reuse the existing primitive rather than creating a parallel timesheet entity. Billing amount derives, not stored redundantly.
mappings.no_loss_check: ERPNext timesheet fields (billable/billed/costing amount, per_billed, parent_project, sales_invoice link) all expressible as derived/linked attrs on the extended TimeEntry; nothing dropped, invoice link goes via sales/accounting integration.
mappings.license_status: GPL-3/LGPL-3: port the model + billing-rollup logic, no verbatim copy.
mappings.risk: Two candidate owners (hr-core TimeEntry vs a new project-time entity). Pick hr-core TimeEntry as canonical to avoid duplicate time stores; business-projects only reads/aggregates.
mappings.reason: Local TimeEntry primitive already exists in hr-core (real method recordTimeEntry); only billable/project/rate fields + rollup are missing, so a contract extension reuses most of the existing service.

mappings.feature: HR / Employee management (employee directory, department, job/designation, org chart, employment lifecycle/onboarding)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Odoo HR is HR-admin-first (HR opens employee record). Re-center: the worker owns their profile (personal-hr self-profile + credentials already does this); HR's directory/org-chart is the management surface reading the same party/employment data, not the primary owner of the person's record.
mappings.person_surface: personal-hr app: worker self-profile, my-credentials, my-department/manager view; person is data owner (PII isolation already enforced).
mappings.management_surface: hr-core: employee directory, department/job hierarchy, org chart, employment status lifecycle; org-side admin + compliance.
mappings.simplification_note: Local already splits this cleanly (hr-core = org employment, personal-hr = self-profile + credentials). Only org-chart visualization and department/job hierarchy entities are thin. Reference Odoo for the field set + org-chart layout algorithm; do not import Odoo code (heavy, GPL).
mappings.no_loss_check: Employee/department/job/employment-status all present or trivially addable; org-chart is a read-model over existing hierarchy. personal-hr adds credential/license/PSV/CEU that Odoo community lacks (stronger than source on the compliance axis).
mappings.license_status: GPL-3/LGPL-3: reference only for field coverage + org-chart UX; local model already authoritative.
mappings.risk: Department/job hierarchy may need to defer to org-core-service (which owns org hierarchy) rather than duplicating in hr-core.
mappings.reason: Local hr-core (partial) + personal-hr (strong, credential/CEU) already cover the substance; source mainly fills org-chart + designation/department detail, so reference not port.

mappings.feature: Leave / time-off (leave types, allocation, accrual plans, requests, approval, balances, public holidays)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Odoo leave is approval-workflow-first (manager dashboard of pending requests). Re-center on the person: 'my balance + request time off' is the spine in personal-hr (see balance, pick dates, submit); manager approval and accrual posting attach behind it.
mappings.person_surface: personal-hr app: my leave balance, request leave, calendar of my time-off + team holidays.
mappings.management_surface: hr-core: leave-type config, accrual-plan rules, allocation, approval queue, balance ledger, public-holiday calendar (via calendar-core).
mappings.simplification_note: hr-core already has LeaveRequest + requestLeave/transitionLeave + LeaveTransition enum. Extend with leave-type, allocation, and accrual-plan entities; reuse the existing request/transition state machine rather than rebuilding. Public holidays come from calendar-core, not a new store.
mappings.no_loss_check: Odoo's leave-type/allocation/accrual-plan/mandatory-day are the missing depth; adding them to the existing LeaveRequest model preserves full leave capability incl. accrual; nothing removed.
mappings.license_status: GPL-3/LGPL-3: port accrual + balance math, no verbatim copy (hr_leave.py is 84KB of Odoo-specific logic).
mappings.risk: Accrual-plan logic is genuinely complex (62KB allocation model); port the rules carefully or it becomes a balance-correctness bug source.
mappings.reason: Core request/transition primitive exists; leave-type + accrual + allocation are the real gap, and Odoo provides the most complete open accrual model worth porting.

mappings.feature: Attendance / clock-in-out + overtime rules
mappings.local_module: hr-core-service (extend; or reuse TimeEntry primitive for clock events)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Odoo attendance is kiosk/terminal-first (employee clocks at a device for the org). Re-center on the worker: check-in/out is a self-service action in personal-hr with my-hours visibility; overtime is computed and shown to the person, then surfaced to management.
mappings.person_surface: personal-hr app: check-in/out, my hours today/this-week, my overtime.
mappings.management_surface: hr-core: attendance log, overtime rulesets, exception/missing-punch reports.
mappings.simplification_note: Model attendance as a thin extension of the existing TimeEntry primitive (clock event = time entry of kind=attendance) instead of a parallel store, unless overtime-rule modeling forces a dedicated entity. ponytail: start with TimeEntry kind, split only if overtime rules need their own table.
mappings.no_loss_check: Check-in/out + overtime computation preserved; Odoo's 38KB overtime-rule engine is enterprise-grade depth that can be filed forward incrementally without losing the basic attendance capability now.
mappings.risk: Overtime-rule engine is large; scope creep risk. Keep MVP to clock-events + simple daily-overtime, file complex rulesets forward.
mappings.reason: No local owner; basic attendance is cheap to add on the existing time primitive, but full overtime-rule parity is heavy, so medium reuse with staged depth.

mappings.feature: Expenses (expense claims, categories, employee reimbursement, approval, GL/journal posting, mileage)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Odoo expense is accountant-first (expense report -> account.move journal). Re-center on the spender: snap-a-receipt, submit, track-reimbursement is the personal-finance-style spine; the journal posting + tax + approval attach as management consequence the person never has to think about.
mappings.person_surface: personal-finance / personal-hr app: capture expense + receipt (storage-service), submit, see reimbursement status.
mappings.management_surface: expense-core-service: category config, approval workflow, GL posting to accounting-core, tax handling, billable-to-project link, reimbursement payment.
mappings.simplification_note: Generate a fresh expense-core via service generator; do not bolt onto hr-core. Reuse storage-service for receipts, accounting-core for journal posting, esign/workflow for approval. Odoo's hr_expense.py is 86KB because it embeds full accounting; we keep expense entity thin and delegate journal posting to accounting-core (single GL owner).
mappings.no_loss_check: Expense claim, category, approval, GL posting, tax, mileage, billable-to-project all preserved via the new contract + accounting-core integration; nothing dropped, accounting concerns delegated to existing GL owner instead of duplicated.
mappings.license_status: GPL-3/LGPL-3: port data model + approval/posting flow, no verbatim copy.
mappings.risk: Must not duplicate ledger logic - expense-core posts events/entries to accounting-core, which remains the single journal owner (DRY/reuse rule).
mappings.reason: Genuine greenfield capability with no local owner; Odoo provides the most complete open expense model and the generator-first path is a clean new service.

mappings.feature: Fixed-asset management + depreciation (asset register, depreciation schedule, capitalization, maintenance, repair, movement/transfer)
mappings.local_module: fleet-core-service (has generic Asset + MaintenanceSchedule) + accounting-core (depreciation journals)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Asset accounting is inherently org/finance-centric (low person-centricity). Person-facing slice = 'assets assigned to me' (my laptop/phone/vehicle, my custody acknowledgement, report-an-issue). Management surface keeps full register + depreciation. Custody/assignment is the person hook.
mappings.person_surface: personal-hr / personal-assets view: assets in my custody, acknowledge receipt, report damage/maintenance need.
mappings.management_surface: asset register: acquisition, depreciation schedule (straight-line/WDV), capitalization, maintenance/repair, movement/transfer, disposal; depreciation posts to accounting-core.
mappings.simplification_note: fleet-core already owns a generic Asset + MaintenanceSchedule entity; extend that or factor an asset-core, rather than a brand-new silo. Reuse maintenance machinery from fleet-core; reuse accounting-core for depreciation journals. ERPNext gives the cleanest depreciation-schedule model to port.
mappings.no_loss_check: Asset, depreciation schedule, capitalization, maintenance, repair, movement, disposal all preserved; depreciation posting delegated to accounting-core (no duplicate ledger). Custody/assignment added as the person dimension - net feature gain.
mappings.license_status: GPL-3 (ERPNext) / LGPL-3 (Odoo maintenance): port the depreciation + movement model, no verbatim copy.
mappings.risk: Ownership decision: extend fleet-core (already has Asset+Maintenance) vs new asset-core. fleet-core Asset may be vehicle-biased; check before forcing fixed-asset accounting into it (rolling-update: extend, don't fork).
mappings.reason: Local has a generic Asset + maintenance scaffold in fleet-core but no depreciation/capitalization/movement; ERPNext's mature asset lifecycle is high-value to port onto the existing primitive.

mappings.feature: Payroll (salary structure, salary slip, pay run, statutory deductions)
mappings.local_module: absent (future payroll-core; consumes hr-core compensation + attendance/leave, posts to accounting-core)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Payroll is the most org/statutory-centric ERP capability. Person slice = 'my payslips / my pay history / my deductions' (read-only, personal-finance style). Full pay-run engine stays management-only.
mappings.person_surface: personal-hr app: my payslips, YTD earnings/deductions, tax docs (W2/1099 already in accounting-core).
mappings.management_surface: payroll-core (future): salary structure, pay-run, statutory deduction/tax tables, payslip generation, bank file export.
mappings.simplification_note: Do NOT scaffold now. Odoo community has no payroll (Enterprise-only); ERPNext payroll lives in the separate hrms app not cloned here; vista PAID is M-code, reference-only. hr-core already has Compensation + PayFrequency + W2/1099 in accounting-core, covering the compensation primitive. Payroll engine is jurisdiction-heavy - file forward (v1.1+) with reference notes, do not build speculatively. ponytail: YAGNI until a real payroll requirement lands.
mappings.no_loss_check: Compensation record + pay frequency + W2/1099 tax forms already exist locally (hr-core + accounting-core). Full pay-run/statutory engine is filed forward, not lost; no current capability removed.
mappings.license_status: ERPNext GPL-3 (hrms not in corpus); vista PAID permissive but M-code (reference-only).
mappings.risk: Statutory/jurisdiction complexity makes payroll a poor speculative build; premature scaffolding would create a defective unmaintained mold.
mappings.reason: No mappable source in this corpus (Odoo community lacks it, ERPNext hrms not cloned), high jurisdictional complexity, and local compensation primitives already cover the near-term need - reference-only and defer.

mappings.feature: Recruitment / applicant tracking (job posting, candidate pipeline, stages, interview)
mappings.local_module: absent (could reuse builder-core forms + workflow-core pipeline + tasks-core)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Recruitment is candidate-journey-shaped (apply -> screen -> interview -> offer), which is naturally person-centric for the candidate. But it is out of the core delivery-people scope and largely expressible via existing primitives.
mappings.person_surface: candidate-facing apply + status (could be a builder-core form + workflow-core pipeline).
mappings.management_surface: recruiter pipeline (Kanban) - expressible as tasks-core + workflow-core.
mappings.simplification_note: Do not build a dedicated recruitment service. The pipeline is a workflow-core process + builder-core application form + tasks-core stages. Reference Odoo's stage model only. ponytail: compose from existing builder/BPM/tasks rather than a new silo.
mappings.no_loss_check: Recruitment capability remains achievable via builder+BPM+tasks composition; no feature lost, just no dedicated silo. File forward if a vertical needs first-class ATS.
mappings.risk: Low; risk is over-building a silo the platform's builder/BPM already covers.
mappings.reason: Adjacent to people domain but best expressed through existing builder-core + workflow-core + tasks-core composition (builder-BPM-first charter), so low dedicated reuse value.

mappings.feature: Skills / competency management (employee skills, skill levels, skill types)
mappings.local_module: personal-hr-service (credentials/CEU already overlaps the competency axis)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: Odoo skills is appraisal/HR-driven. Local already re-centers competency on the person via personal-hr credentials + CEU (worker owns their license/cert/continuing-education). Generic skill tags would extend that worker-owned profile.
mappings.person_surface: personal-hr app: my skills/credentials/CEU (already worker-owned).
mappings.management_surface: hr-core: skill matrix / competency reporting (thin, optional).
mappings.simplification_note: personal-hr credentials + CEU already cover the professional-competency axis stronger than Odoo for regulated roles. A lightweight generic skill-tag field can extend it; no dedicated skills service needed.
mappings.no_loss_check: Competency tracking preserved and arguably stronger locally (license/PSV/CEU). Generic non-credential skills are an easy additive field; nothing lost.
mappings.risk: Low.
mappings.reason: Local personal-hr already exceeds source on regulated credentials; only generic skill tagging is thin, so reference-only with an additive field.

mappings.feature: Calendar / agenda events on business objects (booking, availabilities, contract/invoice agenda)
mappings.person_centric_reshape: n/a - local calendar-core (iCalendar/FHIR, RRULE, occurrence expansion) + scheduling-service (FHIR Slot booking, double-booking prevention) already exceed Dolibarr's PHP agenda.
mappings.person_surface: personal calendar / scheduling already real-working.
mappings.management_surface: scheduling-service desk metrics/alerts already real-working.
mappings.simplification_note: Reject - local calendar/scheduling is more capable and standards-based (FHIR/iCal) than Dolibarr's bookcal.
mappings.no_loss_check: Booking + availability + recurrence all already present and stronger locally; nothing to gain.
mappings.risk: None.
mappings.reason: Local calendar-core + scheduling-service are stronger than this source row (mis-tagged as a task feature); no reuse.

gaps.feature: Project/milestone/stage envelope around tasks (org PM layer)
gaps.local_evidence: business-projects-service is scaffold-only (README + Helm chart, no src/contracts); personal-tasks-service is scaffold-only. tasks-core has FHIR Task but no project/milestone/stage entity.
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo project_project.py/project_milestone.py/project_task_type.py + ERPNext project/project_template/task_type doctypes give a complete project>milestone>stage>task model with Kanban+timeline+recurrence+dependencies.
gaps.why: tasks-core owns the task primitive but there is no org project/milestone/stage owner; business-projects-service is empty. This is the central delivery gap.
steps: 1. Author business-projects-service/specs/projects.tsp with Project, Milestone, Stage (ProjectStage), ProjectTemplate; link tasks via tasks-core Task ref (no duplicate task store).
2. Run service generator (service.playbook.json + service-business template) to scaffold the service from the contract.
3. Generate projects-sdk via sdk-emit; wire business-projects + personal-tasks apps to it.
4. business-projects-service is a read-model + project/milestone owner; tasks-core remains the single Task entity owner (rolling-update, no parallel store).
5. Add migration + service-owned seed (service-seed-emit) with real project/milestone/task demo rows.
gaps.no_loss_check: All Odoo+ERPNext PM entities (project, milestone, stage, template, recurrence, dependency, progress) covered; sale/stock/mrp couplings filed forward to integration edges, not dropped.

gaps.feature: Expenses (claims + reimbursement + GL posting)
gaps.local_evidence: No expense/reimbursement service exists (ls of backend/services shows none). accounting-core can receive journal postings.
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo hr_expense.py (86KB) + account_move.py provide full claim->approval->journal->reimbursement, including tax, mileage, and project-billable links.
gaps.why: Expense management is a named taxonomy feature (erp.people.expenses) with zero local owner and high real-world demand.
steps: 1. Author expense.tsp: ExpenseClaim, ExpenseLine, ExpenseCategory, approval state machine; receipt ref to storage-service; billable-to-project ref.
2. Generate expense-core-service via service generator (service-core or service-personal+business pair if triplet-split criteria met).
3. Delegate journal posting to accounting-core via event/contract (single GL owner; no duplicate ledger logic).
4. Wire approval via workflow-core; receipts via storage-service.
5. Generate expense-sdk; build person-facing capture in personal-finance/personal-hr and management surface in business-* app.
gaps.no_loss_check: Claim, category, approval, tax, mileage, GL posting, billable-to-project all preserved; accounting delegated to accounting-core, not duplicated; nothing dropped.

gaps.feature: Fixed-asset depreciation + capitalization + movement (beyond fleet's generic Asset)
gaps.local_evidence: fleet-core-service has a generic Asset + MaintenanceSchedule (RegisterAssetInput, ScheduleMaintenanceInput) but no depreciation, capitalization, or movement/transfer; no fixed-asset accounting.
gaps.external_evidence: ERPNext assets module: Asset, Asset Depreciation Schedule, Asset Capitalization, Asset Maintenance, Asset Repair, Asset Movement doctypes with depreciation posting; Odoo maintenance.py for equipment maintenance.
gaps.best_source: erpnext (assets) for depreciation/capitalization/movement; odoo maintenance for equipment maintenance
gaps.suggested_mode: port-adapt onto fleet-core Asset or factor asset-core
gaps.why: fleet-core has the Asset primitive but lacks the fixed-asset financial lifecycle (depreciation, capitalization, transfer, disposal).
steps: 1. Decide owner: extend fleet-core Asset (if vehicle-bias is removable) or factor an asset-core - check fleet Asset model first (rolling-update, no fork).
2. Add DepreciationSchedule, Capitalization, AssetMovement, custody/assignment to the chosen contract (.tsp).
3. Delegate depreciation journal posting to accounting-core (single GL owner).
4. Add person dimension: assets-in-my-custody + acknowledge-receipt in personal surface.
5. Regenerate SDK + seed real asset/depreciation demo rows.
gaps.no_loss_check: Asset register, depreciation, capitalization, maintenance, repair, movement, disposal preserved; depreciation posting delegated to accounting-core; custody adds the person axis - net gain.

gaps.feature: Leave-type config + accrual plans + allocation depth
gaps.local_evidence: hr-core has LeaveRequest + LeaveTransition + requestLeave/transitionLeave but no leave-type, allocation, or accrual-plan entities (hr.tsp shows only LeaveRequest/CreateLeaveBody/TransitionLeaveBody).
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo hr_holidays: hr_leave_type.py (37KB), hr_leave_allocation.py (62KB), hr_leave_accrual_plan.py + accrual_plan_level - full accrual/allocation/balance engine.
gaps.why: Leave state machine exists but balance/accrual depth (the compliance-critical part) is missing.
steps: 1. Extend hr.tsp with LeaveType, LeaveAllocation, AccrualPlan(+Level) models + balance read endpoint.
2. Port Odoo accrual/balance math into hr-domain.service (no verbatim copy).
3. Reuse existing requestLeave/transitionLeave state machine; do not fork.
4. Pull public holidays from calendar-core, not a new store.
5. Regenerate hr-sdk; add my-balance + request-leave to personal-hr app.
gaps.no_loss_check: Leave-type/allocation/accrual/mandatory-day all added onto existing request model; full leave + accrual capability preserved; nothing removed.

gaps.feature: Timesheet billable/costing + project linkage + billing rollup
gaps.local_evidence: hr-core TimeEntry + recordTimeEntry exist but RecordTimeEntryBody has no billable flag, costing rate, or project/task ref (hr.tsp).
gaps.external_evidence: ERPNext timesheet.json: billable/billed/costing amount, per_billed, parent_project, sales_invoice; Odoo hr_timesheet project_task.py with analytic costing.
gaps.why: Time primitive exists but billable/costing/project-link (the project-delivery value) is missing.
steps: 1. Add taskRef, projectRef, billable, costingRate to TimeEntry in hr.tsp.
2. business-projects aggregates billable hours -> rollup to sales/accounting via integration.
3. Person-facing per-task timer + my-week in personal-tasks; approval in management surface.
4. Regenerate hr-sdk.
gaps.no_loss_check: ERPNext billable/billed/costing/invoice-link expressible as derived/linked attrs; invoice link via sales/accounting integration; nothing lost.

gaps.feature: Attendance clock-in/out + overtime
gaps.local_evidence: No attendance/clock entity in hr.tsp or any service.
gaps.external_evidence: Odoo hr_attendance.py (37KB) + overtime rule engine (38KB).
gaps.why: No local attendance owner; basic clock-in/out is a common people-ops need.
target_files: curaos/backend/services/hr-core-service/specs/hr.tsp (attendance kind on TimeEntry, or Attendance model)
steps: 1. Add check-in/out as TimeEntry kind=attendance, or a thin Attendance model if overtime rules demand it.
2. MVP: clock events + daily overtime; file complex overtime rulesets forward.
3. Self-service check-in/out + my-hours in personal-hr.
4. Regenerate hr-sdk.
gaps.no_loss_check: Clock-in/out + overtime computation preserved; Odoo's full overtime-rule engine filed forward incrementally; basic capability not lost.

enrichment.enrichment: Add project/milestone context, stage (kanban column), recurrence, effort/progress, task templates on top of the existing FHIR Task primitive - enriching the strong local task entity with the PM dimensions Odoo/ERPNext carry, without losing FHIR-Task interop.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Person keeps a single 'my tasks' stream; project/milestone/stage is context attached per task. Manager view rolls the same tasks up by project - one task entity, two re-centered surfaces.

enrichment.enrichment: Add a billable per-task timer + my-week timesheet summary so logging hours is a by-product of doing the task, feeding hr-core TimeEntry. Already has list/board/calendar/analytics - add the time-capture surface.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Worker captures time inline while working a task and reviews a weekly summary they own; approval/billing happens downstream without the person filling a blank grid.

enrichment.enrichment: Deepen LeaveRequest with leave-type + accrual-plan + allocation/balance; extend TimeEntry with billable/costing/project-link; add attendance clock events. The compensation + leave + time primitives already exist - enrich them with Odoo's accrual/balance and billable depth.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Worker sees 'my balance / my hours / my time-off' as the spine; HR config (leave types, accrual rules, approval) is the management surface over the same data.

enrichment.enrichment: personal-hr already exceeds Odoo on regulated credentials (license/DEA/NPI/PSV/CEU). Add generic non-credential skill tags + assets-in-my-custody view, surfacing the worker-owned competency + asset-custody dimensions.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Worker owns one profile spanning credentials, skills, CEU, and assigned assets; HR's competency matrix and asset register read from it rather than owning it.

enrichment.enrichment: fleet-core has generic Asset + MaintenanceSchedule. Enrich with depreciation schedule, capitalization, movement/transfer, custody assignment to cover fixed-asset accounting beyond vehicles, posting depreciation to accounting-core.
enrichment.person_centric_angle: Person sees the assets in their custody and acknowledges receipt / reports issues; the finance team keeps the full depreciation register over the same asset records.

enrichment.enrichment: Position accounting-core as the single GL owner that receives journal postings from new expense-core (reimbursement) and asset depreciation - enriching its journal/double-entry capability as the posting target rather than letting those services duplicate ledger logic.
enrichment.source: odoo hr_expense account_move + erpnext asset depreciation posting
enrichment.person_centric_angle: The person never touches journals; expense reimbursement and asset depreciation post automatically to the GL behind their simple 'submit expense' / 'acknowledge asset' actions.

mappings.feature: Patient registration + demographics + identifiers (MRN, multi-ID, address hierarchy, person/contact)
